units.text: Enter the DUKE, EGEON, JAILER, and other attendants

units.speaker: EGEON
units.text:
Proceed, Solinus, to procure my fall,
And by the doom of death end woes and all.

units.speaker: DUKE
units.text:
Merchant of Syracuse, plead no more.
I am not partial to infringe our laws.
The enmity and discord which of late
Sprung from the rancorous outrage of your duke
To merchants, our well-dealing countrymen,
Who, wanting guilders to redeem their lives,
Have seal’d his rigorous statutes with their bloods,
Excludes all pity from our threatening looks.
For, since the mortal and intestine jars
'Twixt thy seditious countrymen and us,
It hath in solemn synods been decreed
Both by the Syracusians and ourselves,
To admit no traffic to our adverse towns.
Nay, more, if any born at Ephesus
Be seen at any Syracusian marts and fairs;
Again, if any Syracusian born
Come to the bay of Ephesus, he dies,
His goods confiscate to the Duke’s dispose,
Unless a thousand marks be levièd
To quit the penalty and to ransom him.
Thy substance, valued at the highest rate,
Cannot amount unto a hundred marks;
Therefore by law thou art condemned to die.

units.speaker: EGEON
units.text:
Yet this my comfort: when your words are done,
My woes end likewise with the evening sun.

units.speaker: DUKE
units.text:
Well, Syracusian, say in brief the cause
Why thou dep-artedst from thy native home
And for what cause thou camest to Ephesus.

units.speaker: EGEON
units.text:
A heavier task could not have been imposed
Than I to speak my griefs unspeakable;
Yet, that the world may witness that my end
Was wrought by nature, not by vile offense,
I’ll utter what my sorrow gives me leave.
In Syracusa was I born, and wed
Unto a woman happy but for me,
And by me, had not our hap been bad.
With her I lived in joy. Our wealth increased
By prosperous voyages I often made
To Epidamnum, till my factor’s death
And the great care of goods at random left
Drew me from kind embracements of my spouse;
From whom my absence was not six months old
Before herself— almost at fainting under
The pleasing punishment that women bear—
Had made provision for her following me
And soon and safe arrivèd where I was.
There had she not been long but she became
A joyful mother of two goodly sons,
And, which was strange, the one so like the other
As could not be distinguished but by names.
That very hour, and in the selfsame inn,
A meaner woman was deliverèd
Of such a burden, male twins, both alike.
Those, for their parents were exceeding poor,
I bought and brought up to attend my sons.
My wife, not meanly proud of two such boys,
Made daily motions for our home return.
Unwilling, I agreed. Alas, too soon
We came aboard.
A league from Epidamnum had we sailed
Before the always-wind-obeying deep
Gave any tragic instance of our harm;
But longer did we not retain much hope;
For what obscured light the heavens did grant
Did but convey unto our fearful minds
A doubtful warrant of immediate death,
Which though myself would gladly have embraced,
Yet the incessant weepings of my wife,
Weeping before for what she saw must come,
And piteous plainings of the pretty babes,
That mourned for fashion, ignorant what to fear,
Forced me to seek delays for them and me.
And this it was, for other means was none:
The sailors sought for safety by our boat
And left the ship, then sinking-ripe, to us.
My wife, more careful for the latter-born,
Had fastened him unto a small spare mast,
Such as seafaring men provide for storms.
To him one of the other twins was bound,
Whilst I had been like heedful of the other.
The children thus disposed, my wife and I,
Fixing our eyes on whom our care was fixed,
Fastened ourselves at either end the mast
And, floating straight, obedient to the stream,
Was carried towards Corinth, as we thought.
At length the sun, gazing upon the earth,
Dispersed those vapors that offended us,
And by the benefit of his wished light
The seas waxed calm, and we discoverèd
Two ships from far, making amain to us,
Of Corinth that, of Epidaurus this.
But ere they came,— O, let me say no more!
Gather the sequel by that went before.

units.speaker: DUKE
units.text:
Nay, forward, old man. Do not break off so,
For we may pity though not pardon thee.

units.speaker: EGEON
units.text:
O, had the gods done so, I had not now
Worthily termed them merciless to us.
For, ere the ships could meet by twice five leagues,
We were encountered by a mighty rock,
Which being violently borne upon,
Our helpful ship was splitted in the midst;
So that, in this unjust divorce of us,
Fortune had left to both of us alike
What to delight in, what to sorrow for.
Her part, poor soul, seeming as burdenèd
With lesser weight, but not with lesser woe,
Was carried with more speed before the wind,
And in our sight they three were taken up
By fishermen of Corinth, as we thought.
At length, another ship had seized on us
And, knowing whom it was their hap to save,
Gave healthful welcome to their shipwracked guests,
And would have reft the fishers of their prey
Had not their bark been very slow of sail;
And therefore homeward did they bend their course.
Thus have you heard me severed from my bliss;
That by misfortunes was my life prolonged
To tell sad stories of my own mishaps.

units.speaker: DUKE
units.text:
And for the sake of them thou sorrowest for,
Do me the favour to dilate at full
What hath befall'n of them and thee till now.

units.speaker: EGEON
units.text:
My youngest boy, and yet my eldest care,
At eighteen years became inquisitive
After his brother, and importuned me
That his attendant— so his case was like,
Reft of his brother, but retained his name—
Might bear him company in the quest of him,
Whom whilst I laboured of a love to see,
I hazarded the loss of whom I loved.
Five summers have I spent in farthest Greece,
Roaming clean through the bounds of Asia,
And, coasting homeward, came to Ephesus,
Hopeless to find, yet loath to leave unsought
Or that or any place that harbors men.
But here must end the story of my life;
And happy were I in my timely death
Could all my travels warrant me they live.

units.speaker: DUKE
units.text:
Hapless Egeon, whom the fates have marked
To bear the extremity of dire mishap,
Now, trust me, were it not against our laws,
Against my crown, my oath, my dignity,
Which princes, would they, may not disannul,
My soul would sue as advocate for thee.
But though thou art adjudgèd to the death,
And passèd sentence may not be recalled
But to our honour’s great disparagement,
Yet will I favor thee in what I can.
Therefore, merchant, I’ll limit thee this day
To seek thy life by beneficial help.
Try all the friends thou hast in Ephesus;
Beg thou, or borrow, to make up the sum,
And live. If no, then thou art doom’d to die.—
Jailer, take him to thy custody.

units.speaker: JAILER
units.text: I will, my lord.

units.speaker: EGEON
units.text:
Hopeless and helpless doth Egeon wend,
But to procrastinate his lifeless end.
Exeunt
Enter ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE, DROMIO OF SYRACUSE, and FIRST MERCHANT

units.speaker: FIRST MERCHANT
units.text:
Therefore give out you are of Epidamnum,
Lest that your goods too soon be confiscate.
This very day a Syracusian merchant
Is apprehended for arrival here
And, not being able to buy out his life,
According to the statute of the town
Dies ere the weary sun set in the west.
There is your money that I had to keep.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
Go bear it to the Centaur, where we host,
And stay there, Dromio, till I come to thee.
Within this hour it will be dinnertime.
Till that, I’ll view the manners of the town,
Peruse the traders, gaze upon the buildings,
And then return and sleep within mine inn,
For with long travel I am stiff and weary.
Get thee away.

units.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
Many a man would take you at your word
And go indeed, having so good a mean.
Exit DROMIO OF SYRACUSE

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
A trusty villain, sir, that very oft,
When I am dull with care and melancholy,
Lightens my humor with his merry jests.
What, will you walk with me about the town
And then go to my inn and dine with me?

units.speaker: FIRST MERCHANT
units.text:
I am invited, sir, to certain merchants,
Of whom I hope to make much benefit.
I crave your pardon. Soon at five o'clock,
Please you, I’ll meet with you upon the mart
And afterward consort you till bedtime.
My present business calls me from you now.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
Farewell till then. I will go lose myself
And wander up and down to view the city.

units.speaker: FIRST MERCHANT
units.text: Sir, I commend you to your own content.
Exit FIRST MERCHANT exits.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
He that commends me to mine own content
Commends me to the thing I cannot get.
I to the world am like a drop of water
That in the ocean seeks another drop,
Who, falling there to find his fellow forth,
Unseen, inquisitive, confounds himself.
So I, to find a mother and a brother,
In quest of them, unhappy, lose myself.
Enter DROMIO OF EPHESUS

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
Here comes the almanac of my true date.—
What now? How chance thou art returned so soon?

units.speaker: DROMIO OF EPHESUS
ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
Stop in your wind, sir. Tell me this, I pray:
Where have you left the money that I gave you?

units.speaker: DROMIO OF EPHESUS
units.text:
O, sixpence, that I had o' Wednesday last
To pay the saddler for my mistress' crupper?
The saddler had it, sir; I kept it not.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
I am not in a sportive humor now.
Tell me, and dally not: where is the money?
We being strangers here, how dar’st thou trust
So great a charge from thine own custody?

units.speaker: DROMIO OF EPHESUS
units.text:
I pray you, jest, sir, as you sit at dinner.
I from my mistress come to you in post;
If I return, I shall be post indeed,
For she will scour your fault upon my pate.
Methinks your maw, like mine, should be your clock,
And strike you home without a messenger.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
Come, Dromio, come, these jests are out of season.
Reserve them till a merrier hour than this.
Where is the gold I gave in charge to thee?

units.speaker: DROMIO OF EPHESUS
units.text: To me, sir? Why, you gave no gold to me!

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
Come on, sir knave, have done your foolishness,
And tell me how thou hast disposed thy charge.

units.speaker: DROMIO OF EPHESUS
units.text:
My charge was but to fetch you from the mart
Home to your house, the Phoenix, sir, to dinner.
My mistress and her sister stays for you.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
Now, as I am a Christian, answer me
In what safe place you have bestowed my money,
Or I shall break that merry sconce of yours
That stands on tricks when I am undisposed.
Where is the thousand marks thou hadst of me?

units.speaker: DROMIO OF EPHESUS
units.text:
I have some marks of yours upon my pate,
Some of my mistress' marks upon my shoulders,
But not a thousand marks between you both.
If I should pay your worship those again,
Perchance you will not bear them patiently.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
units.text: Thy mistress' marks? what mistress, slave, hast thou?

units.speaker: DROMIO OF EPHESUS
units.text:
Your worship’s wife, my mistress at the Phoenix,
She that doth fast till you come home to dinner
And prays that you will hie you home to dinner.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
What, wilt thou flout me thus unto my face,
Being forbid? There, take you that, sir knave. [beats
DROMIO OF EPHESUS]

units.speaker: DROMIO OF EPHESUS
units.text:
What mean you, sir? For God’s sake, hold your hands.
Nay, an you will not, sir, I’ll take my heels.
Exit DROMIO OF EPHESUS

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
Upon my life, by some device or other
The villain is o'erraught of all my money.
They say this town is full of cozenage,
As nimble jugglers that deceive the eye,
Dark-working sorcerers that change the mind,
Soul-killing witches that deform the body,
Disguised cheaters, prating mountebanks,
And many suchlike liberties of sin.
If it prove so, I will be gone the sooner.
I’ll to the Centaur to go seek this slave.
I greatly fear my money is not safe.
Exit
Enter ADRIANA and LUCIANA

units.speaker: ADRIANA
units.text:
Neither my husband nor the slave returned
That in such haste I sent to seek his master?
Sure, Luciana, it is two o'clock.

units.speaker: LUCIANA
units.text:
Perhaps some merchant hath invited him,
And from the mart he’s somewhere gone to dinner.
Good sister, let us dine and never fret.
A man is master of his liberty;
Time is their master, and when they see time
They’ll go or come. If so, be patient, sister.

units.speaker: ADRIANA
units.text: Why should their liberty than ours be more?

units.speaker: LUCIANA
units.text: Because their business still lies out o' door.

units.speaker: ADRIANA
units.text: Look when I serve him so, he takes it ill.

units.speaker: LUCIANA
units.text: O, know he is the bridle of your will.

units.speaker: ADRIANA
units.text: There’s none but asses will be bridled so.

units.speaker: LUCIANA
units.text:
Why, headstrong liberty is lashed with woe.
There’s nothing situate under heaven’s eye
But hath his bound, in earth, in sea, in sky.
The beasts, the fishes, and the wingèd fowls
Are their males' subjects and at their controls.
Man, more divine, the masters of all these,
Lord of the wide world and wild wat'ry seas,
Endued with intellectual sense and souls,
Of more preeminence than fish and fowls,
Are masters to their females, and their lords.
Then let your will attend on their accords.

units.speaker: ADRIANA
units.text: This servitude makes you to keep unwed.

units.speaker: LUCIANA
units.text: Not this, but troubles of the marriage bed.

units.speaker: ADRIANA
units.text: But, were you wedded, you would bear some sway.

units.speaker: LUCIANA
units.text: Ere I learn love, I’ll practice to obey.

units.speaker: ADRIANA
units.text: How if your husband start some otherwhere?

units.speaker: LUCIANA
units.text: Till he come home again, I would forbear.

units.speaker: ADRIANA
units.text:
Patience unmoved! No marvel though she pause;
They can be meek that have no other cause.
A wretched soul, bruised with adversity
We bid be quiet when we hear it cry,
But were we burdened with like weight of pain,
As much or more we should ourselves complain.
So thou, that hast no unkind mate to grieve thee,
With urging helpless patience would relieve me;
But, if thou live to see like right bereft,
This fool-begged patience in thee will be left.

units.speaker: LUCIANA
units.text:
Well, I will marry one day, but to try.
Here comes your man. Now is your husband nigh.
Enter DROMIO OF EPHESUS

units.speaker: ADRIANA
units.text: Say, is your tardy master now at hand?

units.speaker: DROMIO OF EPHESUS
units.text:
Nay, he’s at two hands with me, and that my two ears
can witness.

units.speaker: ADRIANA
units.text: Say, didst thou speak with him? Know’st thou his mind?

units.speaker: DROMIO OF EPHESUS
units.text:
Ay, ay, he told his mind upon mine ear.
Beshrew his hand, I scarce could understand it.

units.speaker: LUCIANA
units.text:
Spake he so doubtfully thou couldst not feel his
meaning?

units.speaker: DROMIO OF EPHESUS
units.text:
Nay, he struck so plainly I could too well feel his
blows, and withal so doubtfully that I could scarce
understand them.

units.speaker: ADRIANA
units.text:
But say, I prithee, is he coming home?
It seems he hath great care to please his wife.

units.speaker: DROMIO OF EPHESUS
units.text: Why, mistress, sure my master is horn mad.
Horn mad, thou villain!

units.speaker: DROMIO OF EPHESUS
units.text:
I mean not cuckold mad,
But sure he is stark mad.
When I desired him to come home to dinner,
He asked me for a thousand marks in gold.
“'Tis dinnertime,” quoth I. “My gold,” quoth he.
“Your meat doth burn,” quoth I. “My gold,” quoth he.
“Will you come?” quoth I. “My gold,” quoth he.
“Where is the thousand marks I gave thee, villain?”
“The pig,” quoth I, “is burned.” “My gold,” quoth he.
“My mistress, sir,” quoth I. “Hang up thy mistress!
I know not thy mistress. Out on thy mistress!”

units.speaker: LUCIANA
units.text: Quoth who?

units.speaker: DROMIO OF EPHESUS
units.text:
Quoth my master.
“I know,” quoth he, “no house, no wife, no mistress.”
So that my errand, due unto my tongue,
I thank him, I bare home upon my shoulders,
For, in conclusion, he did beat me there.

units.speaker: ADRIANA
units.text: Go back again, thou slave, and fetch him home.

units.speaker: DROMIO OF EPHESUS
units.text:
Go back again and be new beaten home?
For God’s sake, send some other messenger.

units.speaker: ADRIANA
units.text: Back, slave, or I will break thy pate across.

units.speaker: DROMIO OF EPHESUS
units.text:
And he will bless that cross with other beating.
Between you, I shall have a holy head.

units.speaker: ADRIANA
units.text: Hence, prating peasant! Fetch thy master home.

units.speaker: DROMIO OF EPHESUS
units.text:
Am I so round with you as you with me,
That like a football you do spurn me thus?
You spurn me hence, and he will spurn me hither.
If I last in this service, you must case me in leather.
Exit DROMIO OF EPHESUS

units.speaker: LUCIANA
units.text: Fie, how impatience loureth in your face.

units.speaker: ADRIANA
units.text:
His company must do his minions grace,
Whilst I at home starve for a merry look.
Hath homely age th' alluring beauty took
From my poor cheek? Then he hath wasted it.
Are my discourses dull? Barren my wit?
If voluble and sharp discourse be marred,
Unkindness blunts it more than marble hard.
Do their gay vestments his affections bait?
That’s not my fault; he’s master of my state.
What ruins are in me that can be found
By him not ruined? Then is he the ground
Of my defeatures. My decayèd fair
A sunny look of his would soon repair.
But, too unruly deer, he breaks the pale
And feeds from home. Poor I am but his stale.

units.speaker: LUCIANA
units.text: Self-harming jealousy, fie, beat it hence.

units.speaker: ADRIANA
units.text:
Unfeeling fools can with such wrongs dispense.
I know his eye doth homage otherwhere,
Or else what lets it but he would be here?
Sister, you know he promised me a chain.
Would that alone o' love he would detain,
So he would keep fair quarter with his bed.
I see the jewel best enamelèd
Will lose his beauty. Yet the gold bides still
That others touch, and often touching will
Wear gold; yet no man that hath a name
By falsehood and corruption doth it shame.
Since that my beauty cannot please his eye,
I’ll weep what’s left away, and weeping die.

units.speaker: LUCIANA
units.text: How many fond fools serve mad jealousy!
Exeunt
Enter ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
The gold I gave to Dromio is laid up
Safe at the Centaur, and the heedful slave
Is wandered forth, in care to seek me out.
By computation and mine host’s report,
I could not speak with Dromio since at first
I sent him from the mart. See, here he comes.
Enter DROMIO OF SYRACUSE

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
How now, sir? is your merry humor altered?
As you love strokes, so jest with me again.
You know no Centaur? You received no gold?
Your mistress sent to have me home to dinner?
My house was at the Phoenix? Wast thou mad,
That thus so madly thou didst answer me?

units.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
units.text: What answer, sir? When spake I such a word?

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
units.text: Even now, even here, not half an hour since.

units.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
I did not see you since you sent me hence,
Home to the Centaur with the gold you gave me.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
Villain, thou didst deny the gold’s receipt
And told’st me of a mistress and a dinner,
For which, I hope, thou felt’st I was displeased.

units.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
I am glad to see you in this merry vein.
What means this jest? I pray you, master, tell me?

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
Yea, dost thou jeer and flout me in the teeth?
Think’st thou I jest? Hold, take thou that and that.
[beats DROMIO OF SYRACUSE

units.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
Hold, sir, for God’s sake! Now your jest is earnest.
Upon what bargain do you give it me?

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
Because that I familiarly sometimes
Do use you for my fool and chat with you,
Your sauciness will jest upon my love
And make a common of my serious hours.
When the sun shines, let foolish gnats make sport,
But creep in crannies when he hides his beams.
If you will jest with me, know my aspect,
And fashion your demeanor to my looks,
Or I will beat this method in your sconce.

units.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
“Sconce” call you it? So you would leave battering, I
had rather have it a “head.” An you use these blows
long, I must get a sconce for my head and ensconce it
too, or else I shall seek my wit in my shoulders. But I
pray, sir, why am I beaten?

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
units.text: Dost thou not know?

units.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
units.text: Nothing, sir, but that I am beaten.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
units.text: Shall I tell you why?

units.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
Ay, sir, and wherefore, for they say every why hath a
wherefore.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
“Why” first: for flouting me; and then “wherefore”: for
urging it the second time to me.

units.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
Was there ever any man thus beaten out of season,
When in the “why” and the “wherefore” is neither rhyme
nor reason?
Well, sir, I thank you.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
units.text: Thank me, sir, for what?

units.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
Marry, sir, for this something that you gave me for
nothing.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
I’ll make you amends next, to give you nothing for
something. But say, sir, is it dinnertime?

units.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
units.text: No, sir, I think the meat wants that I have.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
units.text: In good time, sir, what’s that?

units.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
units.text: Basting.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
units.text: Well, sir, then ’twill be dry.

units.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
units.text: If it be, sir, I pray you, eat none of it.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
units.text: Your reason?

units.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
Lest it make you choleric and purchase me another dry
basting.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
Well, sir, learn to jest in good time. There’s a time
for all things.

units.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
units.text: I durst have denied that before you were so choleric.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
units.text: By what rule, sir?

units.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
Marry, sir, by a rule as plain as the plain bald pate
of Father
Time himself.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
units.text: Let’s hear it.

units.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
There’s no time for a man to recover his hair that
grows bald by nature.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
units.text: May he not do it by fine and recovery?

units.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
Yes, to pay a fine for a periwig, and recover the lost
hair of another man.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
Why is Time such a niggard of hair, being, as it is, so
plentiful an excrement?

units.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
Because it is a blessing that he bestows on beasts, and
what he hath scanted men in hair, he hath given them in
wit.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
units.text: Why, but there’s many a man hath more hair than wit.

units.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
Not a man of those but he hath the wit to lose his
hair.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
Why, thou didst conclude hairy men plain dealers
without wit.

units.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
The plainer dealer, the sooner lost. Yet he loseth it
in a kind of jollity.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
units.text: For what reason?

units.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
units.text: For two, and sound ones too.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
units.text: Nay, not sound, I pray you.

units.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
units.text: Sure ones, then.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
units.text: Nay, not sure, in a thing falsing.

units.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
units.text: Certain ones, then.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
units.text: Name them.

units.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
You would all this time have proved there is no time
for all things.

units.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
Marry, and did, sir: namely, e'en no time to recover
hair lost by nature.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
But your reason was not substantial why there is no
time to recover.

units.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
Thus I mend it: Time himself is bald and therefore, to
the world’s end, will have bald followers.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
I knew ’twould be a bald conclusion:
But soft, who wafts us yonder?
Enter ADRIANA and LUCIANA

units.speaker: ADRIANA
units.text:
Ay, ay, Antipholus, look strange and frown.
Some other mistress hath thy sweet aspects.
I am not Adriana, nor thy wife.
The time was once when thou unurged wouldst vow
That never words were music to thine ear,
That never object pleasing in thine eye,
That never touch well welcome to thy hand,
That never meat sweet-savored in thy taste,
Unless I spake, or looked, or touched, or carved to
thee.
How comes it now, my husband, O, how comes it
That thou art thus estranged from thyself?
Thy "self" I call it, being strange to me,
That, undividable, incorporate,
Am better than thy dear self’s better part.
Ah, do not tear away thyself from me!
For know, my love, as easy mayest thou fall
A drop of water in the breaking gulf,
And take unmingled thence that drop again
Without addition or diminishing,
As take from me thyself and not me too.
How dearly would it touch thee to the quick,
Shouldst thou but hear I were licentious
And that this body, consecrate to thee,
By ruffian lust should be contaminate!
Wouldst thou not spit at me, and spurn at me,
And hurl the name of husband in my face,
And tear the stained skin off my harlot brow,
And from my false hand cut the wedding ring,
And break it with a deep-divorcing vow?
I know thou canst, and therefore see thou do it.
I am possessed with an adulterate blot;
My blood is mingled with the crime of lust;
For if we two be one, and thou play false,
I do digest the poison of thy flesh,
Being strumpeted by thy contagion.
Keep then fair league and truce with thy true bed,
I live disstained, thou undishonorèd.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
Plead you to me, fair dame? I know you not.
In Ephesus I am but two hours old,
As strange unto your town as to your talk,
Who, every word by all my wit being scanned,
Want wit in all one word to understand.

units.speaker: LUCIANA
units.text:
Fie, brother, how the world is changed with you!
When were you wont to use my sister thus?
She sent for you by Dromio home to dinner.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
units.text: By Dromio?

units.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
units.text: By me?

units.speaker: ADRIANA
units.text:
By thee; and this thou didst return from him:
That he did buffet thee and, in his blows,
Denied my house for his, me for his wife.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
Did you converse, sir, with this gentlewoman?
What is the course and drift of your compact?

units.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
units.text: I, sir? I never saw her till this time.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
Villain, thou liest; for even her very words
Didst thou deliver to me on the mart.

units.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
units.text: I never spake with her in all my life.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
How can she thus then call us by our names—
Unless it be by inspiration?

units.speaker: ADRIANA
units.text:
How ill agrees it with your gravity
To counterfeit thus grossly with your slave,
Abetting him to thwart me in my mood.
Be it my wrong you are from me exempt,
But wrong not that wrong with a more contempt.
Come, I will fasten on this sleeve of thine.
Thou art an elm, my husband, I a vine,
Whose weakness, married to thy stronger state,
Makes me with thy strength to communicate.
If aught possess thee from me, it is dross,
Usurping ivy, brier, or idle moss,
Who, all for want of pruning, with intrusion
Infect thy sap and live on thy confusion.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
To me she speaks; she moves me for her theme.
What, was I married to her in my dream?
Or sleep I now and think I hear all this?
What error drives our eyes and ears amiss?
Until I know this sure uncertainty
I’ll entertain the offered fallacy.

units.speaker: LUCIANA
units.text: Dromio, go bid the servants spread for dinner.

units.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
O, for my beads! I cross me for a sinner.
This is the fairy land. O spite of spites!
We talk with goblins, owls, and sprites:
If we obey them not, this will ensue:
They’ll suck our breath, or pinch us black and blue.

units.speaker: LUCIANA
units.text:
Why prat’st thou to thyself and answer’st not?
Dromio—thou, Dromio—thou snail, thou slug, thou sot.

units.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
units.text: I am transformèd, master, am I not?

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
units.text: I think thou art in mind, and so am I.

units.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
units.text: Nay, master, both in mind and in my shape.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
units.text: Thou hast thine own form.

units.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
units.text: No, I am an ape.

units.speaker: LUCIANA
units.text: If thou art changed to aught, ’tis to an ass.

units.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
'Tis true. She rides me, and I long for grass.
'Tis so. I am an ass; else it could never be
But I should know her as well as she knows me.

units.speaker: ADRIANA
units.text:
Come, come, no longer will I be a fool,
To put the finger in the eye and weep
Whilst man and master laugh my woes to scorn.
Come, sir, to dinner. —Dromio, keep the gate. —
Husband, I’ll dine above with you today,
And shrive you of a thousand idle pranks.
Sirrah, if any ask you for your master,
Say he dines forth, and let no creature enter.—
Come, sister.—Dromio, play the porter well.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
Am I in earth, in heaven, or in hell?
Sleeping or waking, mad or well-advised?
Known unto these, and to myself disguised!
I’ll say as they say, and persever so,
And in this mist at all adventures go.

units.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
units.text: Master, shall I be porter at the gate?

units.speaker: ADRIANA
units.text: Ay; and let none enter, lest I break your pate.

units.speaker: LUCIANA
units.text: Come, come, Antipholus, we dine too late.
Exeunt
Enter ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS, DROMIO OF EPHESUS, ANGELO, and BALTHASAR

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
units.text:
Good Signior Angelo, you must excuse us all;
My wife is shrewish when I keep not hours.
Say that I lingered with you at your shop
To see the making of her carcanet,
And that tomorrow you will bring it home.
But here’s a villain that would face me down
He met me on the mart, and that I beat him
And charged him with a thousand marks in gold,
And that I did deny my wife and house.—
Thou drunkard, thou, what didst thou mean by this?

units.speaker: DROMIO OF EPHESUS
units.text:
Say what you will, sir, but I know what I know.
That you beat me at the mart I have your hand to show;
If the skin were parchment and the blows you gave were
ink,
Your own handwriting would tell you what I think.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
units.text: I think thou art an ass.

units.speaker: DROMIO OF EPHESUS
units.text:
Marry, so it doth appear
By the wrongs I suffer and the blows I bear.
I should kick being kicked; and, being at that pass,
You would keep from my heels and beware of an ass.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
units.text:
You’re sad, Signior Balthasar. Pray God our cheer
May answer my good will and your good welcome here.

units.speaker: BALTHASAR
units.text: I hold your dainties cheap, sir, and your welcome dear.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
units.text:
O Signior Balthasar, either at flesh or fish
A table full of welcome make scarce one dainty dish.

units.speaker: BALTHASAR
units.text: Good meat, sir, is common; that every churl affords.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
units.text: And welcome more common, for that’s nothing but words.

units.speaker: BALTHASAR
units.text: Small cheer and great welcome makes a merry feast.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
units.text:
Ay, to a niggardly host and more sparing guest.
But though my cates be mean, take them in good part.
Better cheer may you have, but not with better heart.
But soft! My door is lock’d. [to DROMIO] Go, bid them
let us in.

units.speaker: DROMIO OF EPHESUS
units.text: Maud, Bridget, Marian, Ciceley, Gillian, Ginn!

units.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
[within] Mome, malt-horse, capon, coxcomb, idiot,
patch!
Either get thee from the door or sit down at the hatch.
Dost thou conjure for wenches, that thou call’st for
such store
When one is one too many? Go, get thee from the door.

units.speaker: DROMIO OF EPHESUS
units.text:
What patch is made our porter? My master stays in the
street.

units.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
[within] Let him walk from whence he came, lest he
catch cold on ’s feet.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
units.text: Who talks within there? Ho, open the door.

units.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
[within] Right, sir, I’ll tell you when an you tell me
wherefore.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
units.text: Wherefore? For my dinner. I have not dined today.

units.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
[within] Nor today here you must not. Come again when
you may.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
units.text: What art thou that keep’st me out from the house I owe?

units.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
[within] The porter for this time, sir, and my name is
Dromio.

units.speaker: DROMIO OF EPHESUS
units.text:
O villain, thou hast stolen both mine office and my
name!
The one ne'er got me credit, the other mickle blame.
If thou hadst been Dromio today in my place,
Thou wouldst have changed thy face for a name, or thy
name
for an ass.

units.speaker: LUCE
units.text:
[within] What a coil is there, Dromio! Who are those at
the gate?

units.speaker: DROMIO OF EPHESUS
units.text: Let my master in, Luce.

units.speaker: LUCE
units.text:
[within] Faith, no, he comes too late,
And so tell your master.

units.speaker: DROMIO OF EPHESUS
units.text:
O Lord, I must laugh.
Have at you with a proverb: shall I set in my staff?

units.speaker: LUCE
units.text:
[within] Have at you with another: that’s—When, can you
tell?

units.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
[within] If thy name be called “Luce,” Luce, thou hast
answered him well.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
units.text: Do you hear, you minion? You’ll let us in, I hope?

units.speaker: LUCE
units.text: [within] I thought to have asked you.

units.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
units.text: [within] And you said no.

units.speaker: DROMIO OF EPHESUS
units.text: So, come, help. Well struck! There was blow for blow.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
units.text: Thou baggage, let me in.

units.speaker: LUCE
units.text: [within) Can you tell for whose sake?

units.speaker: DROMIO OF EPHESUS
units.text: Master, knock the door hard.

units.speaker: LUCE
units.text: [within] Let him knock till it ache.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
units.text: You’ll cry for this, minion, if I beat the door down.

units.speaker: LUCE
units.text:
[within] What needs all that, and a pair of stocks in
the town?

units.speaker: ADRIANA
units.text:
[within] Who is that at the door that keeps all this
noise?

units.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
[within] By my troth, your town is troubled with unruly
boys.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
units.text: Are you there, wife? You might have come before.

units.speaker: ADRIANA
units.text:
[within] Your wife, sir knave? Go, get you from the
door.

units.speaker: DROMIO OF EPHESUS
units.text: If you went in pain, master, this knave would go sore.

units.speaker: ANGELO
units.text:
Here is neither cheer, sir, nor welcome. We would
fain have either.

units.speaker: BALTHASAR
units.text: In debating which was best, we shall part with neither.

units.speaker: DROMIO OF EPHESUS
units.text:
They stand at the door, master. Bid them welcome
hither.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
units.text: There is something in the wind, that we cannot get in.

units.speaker: DROMIO OF EPHESUS
units.text:
You would say so, master, if your garments were thin.
Your cake there is warm within; you stand here in the
cold.
It would make a man mad as a buck to be so bought and
sold.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
units.text: Go, fetch me something: I’ll break ope the gate.

units.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
[within] Break any breaking here, and I’ll break your
knave’s pate.

units.speaker: DROMIO OF EPHESUS
units.text:
A man may break a word with you, sir, and words are but
wind,
Ay, and break it in your face, so he break it not
behind.

units.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
[within] It seems thou want’st breaking. Out upon thee,
hind!

units.speaker: DROMIO OF EPHESUS
units.text:
Here’s too much “out upon thee!” I pray thee, let me
in.

units.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
[within] Ay, when fowls have no feathers and fish have
no fin.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
units.text: Well, I’ll break in. Go, borrow me a crow.

units.speaker: DROMIO OF EPHESUS
units.text:
A crow without feather? Master, mean you so?
For a fish without a fin, there’s a fowl without a
feather.—
[To DROMIO OF SYRACUSE] If a crow help us in, sirrah,
we’ll pluck a crow together.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
units.text: Go, get thee gone. Fetch me an iron crow.

units.speaker: BALTHASAR
units.text:
Have patience, sir. O, let it not be so.
Herein you war against your reputation,
And draw within the compass of suspect
Th' unviolated honor of your wife.
Once this: your long experience of her wisdom,
Her sober virtue, years, and modesty
Plead on her part some cause to you unknown.
And doubt not, sir, but she will well excuse
Why at this time the doors are made against you.
Be ruled by me; depart in patience,
And let us to the Tiger all to dinner,
And about evening come yourself alone
To know the reason of this strange restraint.
If by strong hand you offer to break in
Now in the stirring passage of the day,
A vulgar comment will be made of it;
And that supposèd by the common rout
Against your yet ungallèd estimation
That may with foul intrusion enter in
And dwell upon your grave when you are dead;
For slander lives upon succession,
Forever housèd where it gets possession.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
units.text:
You have prevailed. I will depart in quiet
And, in despite of mirth, mean to be merry.
I know a wench of excellent discourse,
Pretty and witty, wild and yet, too, gentle.
There will we dine. This woman that I mean,
My wife—but, I protest, without desert—
Hath oftentimes upbraided me withal;
To her will we to dinner. [to ANGELO] Get you home
And fetch the chain; by this I know ’tis made.
Bring it, I pray you, to the Porpentine,
For there’s the house. That chain will I bestow—
Be it for nothing but to spite my wife—
Upon mine hostess there. Good sir, make haste.
Since mine own doors refuse to entertain me,
I’ll knock elsewhere, to see if they’ll disdain me.

units.speaker: ANGELO
units.text: I’ll meet you at that place some hour hence.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
units.text: Do so. This jest shall cost me some expense.
Exeunt
Enter LUCIANA and ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE

units.speaker: LUCIANA
units.text:
And may it be that you have quite forgot
A husband’s office? Shall, Antipholus,
Even in the spring of love thy love-springs rot?
Shall love, in building, grow so ruinous?
If you did wed my sister for her wealth,
Then for her wealth’s sake use her with more kindness.
Or if you like elsewhere, do it by stealth—
Muffle your false love with some show of blindness.
Let not my sister read it in your eye;
Be not thy tongue thy own shame’s orator;
Look sweet, be fair, become disloyalty;
Apparel vice like virtue’s harbinger.
Bear a fair presence, though your heart be tainted.
Teach sin the carriage of a holy saint.
Be secret-false. What need she be acquainted?
What simple thief brags of his own attaint?
'Tis double wrong to truant with your bed
And let her read it in thy looks at board.
Shame hath a bastard fame, well managèd;
Ill deeds is doubled with an evil word.
Alas, poor women, make us but believe,
Being compact of credit, that you love us.
Though others have the arm, show us the sleeve;
We in your motion turn, and you may move us.
Then, gentle brother, get you in again.
Comfort my sister, cheer her, call her wife.
'Tis holy sport to be a little vain
When the sweet breath of flattery conquers strife.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
Sweet mistress—what your name is else I know not,
Nor by what wonder you do hit of mine,—
Less in your knowledge and your grace you show not
Than our earth’s wonder, more than earth divine.
Teach me, dear creature, how to think and speak.
Lay open to my earthy gross conceit,
Smothered in errors, feeble, shallow, weak,
The folded meaning of your words' deceit.
Against my soul’s pure truth why labour you
To make it wander in an unknown field?
Are you a god? would you create me new?
Transform me, then, and to your power I’ll yield.
But if that I am I, then well I know
Your weeping sister is no wife of mine,
Nor to her bed no homage do I owe.
Far more, far more, to you do I decline.
O, train me not, sweet mermaid, with thy note
To drown me in thy sister’s flood of tears.
Sing, Siren, for thyself, and I will dote.
Spread o'er the silver waves thy golden hairs,
And as a bed I’ll take them and there lie,
And in that glorious supposition think
He gains by death that hath such means to die.
Let Love, being light, be drownèd if she sink.

units.speaker: LUCIANA
units.text: What, are you mad that you do reason so?

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
units.text: Not mad, but mated—how, I do not know.

units.speaker: LUCIANA
units.text: It is a fault that springeth from your eye.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
units.text: For gazing on your beams, fair sun, being by.

units.speaker: LUCIANA
units.text: Gaze where you should, and that will clear your sight.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
units.text: As good to wink, sweet love, as look on night.

units.speaker: LUCIANA
units.text: Why call you me “love”? Call my sister so.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
units.text: Thy sister’s sister.

units.speaker: LUCIANA
units.text: That’s my sister.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
No,
It is thyself, mine own self’s better part,
Mine eye’s clear eye, my dear heart’s dearer heart,
My food, my fortune, and my sweet hope’s aim,
My sole earth’s heaven, and my heaven’s claim.

units.speaker: LUCIANA
units.text: All this my sister is, or else should be.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
Call thyself “sister,” sweet, for I am thee.
Thee will I love and with thee lead my life;
Thou hast no husband yet, nor I no wife.
Give me thy hand.

units.speaker: LUCIANA
units.text:
O soft, sir! Hold you still.
I’ll fetch my sister to get her goodwill.
Exit LUCIANA
Enter DROMIO OF SYRACUSE

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
units.text: Why, how now, Dromio. Where runn’st thou so fast?

units.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
Do you know me, sir? Am I Dromio? Am I your man? Am
I myself?

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
units.text: Thou art Dromio, thou art my man, thou art thyself.

units.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
units.text: I am an ass, I am a woman’s man, and besides myself.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
units.text: What woman’s man? And how besides thyself?

units.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
Marry, sir, besides myself I am due to a woman, one
that claims me, one that haunts me, one that will have
me.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
units.text: What claim lays she to thee?

units.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
Marry, sir, such claim as you would lay to your horse;
and she would have me as a beast; not that I being a
beast she would have me, but that she, being a very
beastly creature, lays claim to me.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
units.text: What is she?

units.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
A very reverent body, ay, such a one as a man may not
speak of without he say “sir-reverence.” I have but lean
luck in the match, and yet is she a wondrous fat
marriage.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
units.text: How dost thou mean a “fat marriage”?

units.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
Marry, sir, she’s the kitchen wench, and all grease,
and I know not what use to put her to but to make a lamp
of her and run from her by her own light. I warrant her
rags and the tallow in them will burn a Poland winter.
If she lives till doomsday, she’ll burn a week longer
than the whole world.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
units.text: What complexion is she of?

units.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
Swart like my shoe, but her face nothing like so clean
kept. For why? She sweats a man may go overshoes in the
grime of it.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
units.text: That’s a fault that water will mend.

units.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
units.text: No, sir, ’tis in grain; Noah’s flood could not do it.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
units.text: What’s her name?

units.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
Nell, sir, but her name and three quarters—that’s an
ell and three quarters—will not measure her from hip to
hip.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
units.text: Then she bears some breadth?

units.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
No longer from head to foot than from hip to hip. She
is spherical, like a globe. I could find out countries
in her.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
units.text: In what part of her body stands Ireland?

units.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
Marry, sir, in her buttocks. I found it out by the
bogs.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
units.text: Where Scotland?

units.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
I found it by the barrenness; hard in the palm of the
hand.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
units.text: Where France?

units.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
In her forehead, armed and reverted, making war against
her heir.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
units.text: Where England?

units.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
I looked for the chalky cliffs, but I could find no
whiteness in them. But I guess it stood in her chin, by
the salt rheum that ran between France and it.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
units.text: Where Spain?

units.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
units.text: Faith, I saw it not, but I felt it hot in her breath.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
units.text: Where America, the Indies?

units.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
O, sir, upon her nose, all o'er-embellished with
rubies, carbuncles, sapphires, declining their rich
aspect to the hot breath of Spain, who sent whole
armadas of caracks to be ballast at her nose.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
units.text: Where stood Belgia, the Netherlands?

units.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
O, sir, I did not look so low. To conclude: this drudge
or diviner laid claim to me, call’d me Dromio, swore I
was assured to her, told me what privy marks I had about
me, as the mark of my shoulder, the mole in my neck,
the great wart on my left arm, that I, amazed, ran from
her as a witch. And, I think, if my breast had not been
made of faith, and my heart of steel, She had
transformed me to a curtal dog and made me turn i' th'
wheel.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
Go, hie thee presently. Post to the road.
An if the wind blow any way from shore,
I will not harbor in this town tonight.
If any bark put forth, come to the mart,
Where I will walk till thou return to me.
If every one knows us, and we know none,
'Tis time, I think, to trudge, pack, and be gone.

units.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
As from a bear a man would run for life,
So fly I from her that would be my wife.
Exit DROMIO OF SYRACUSE

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
There’s none but witches do inhabit here,
And therefore ’tis high time that I were hence.
She that doth call me husband, even my soul
Doth for a wife abhor. But her fair sister,
Possessed with such a gentle sovereign grace,
Of such enchanting presence and discourse,
Hath almost made me traitor to myself.
But lest myself be guilty to self wrong,
I’ll stop mine ears against the mermaid’s song.
Enter ANGELO with the chain

units.speaker: ANGELO
units.text: Master Antipholus.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
units.text: Ay, that’s my name.

units.speaker: ANGELO
ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
units.text: What is your will that I shall do with this?

units.speaker: ANGELO
units.text: What please yourself, sir. I have made it for you.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
units.text: Made it for me, sir? I bespoke it not.

units.speaker: ANGELO
units.text:
Not once, nor twice, but twenty times you have.
Go home with it and please your wife withal,
And soon at supper time I’ll visit you
And then receive my money for the chain.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
I pray you, sir, receive the money now,
For fear you ne'er see chain nor money more.

units.speaker: ANGELO
units.text: You are a merry man, sir. Fare you well.
Exit ANGELO

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
What I should think of this I cannot tell,
But this I think: there’s no man is so vain
That would refuse so fair an offered chain.
I see a man here needs not live by shifts
When in the streets he meets such golden gifts.
I’ll to the mart, and there for Dromio stay.
If any ship put out, then straight away.
Exit
Enter SECOND MERCHANT, ANGELO and an OFFICER

units.speaker: SECOND MERCHANT
units.text:
You know since Pentecost the sum is due,
And since I have not much importuned you,
Nor now I had not, but that I am bound
To Persia, and want guilders for my voyage.
Therefore make present satisfaction,
Or I’ll attach you by this officer.

units.speaker: ANGELO
units.text:
Even just the sum that I do owe to you
Is growing to me by Antipholus.
And in the instant that I met with you,
He had of me a chain. At five o'clock
I shall receive the money for the same.
Pleaseth you walk with me down to his house,
I will discharge my bond and thank you too.
Enter ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS and DROMIO OF EPHESUS from the COURTESAN'S house

units.speaker: COURTESAN S OFFICER
units.text: That labor may you save. See where he comes.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
units.text:
While I go to the goldsmith’s house, go thou
And buy a rope’s end. That will I bestow
Among my wife and her confederates
For locking me out of my doors by day.
But soft. I see the goldsmith. Get thee gone.
Buy thou a rope, and bring it home to me.

units.speaker: DROMIO OF EPHESUS
units.text: I buy a thousand pound a year! I buy a rope!
Exit DROMIO OF EPHESUS

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
units.text:
[to ANGELO] A man is well holp up that trusts to you!
I promisèd your presence and the chain,
But neither chain nor goldsmith came to me.
Belike you thought our love would last too long
If it were chained together, and therefore came not.

units.speaker: ANGELO
units.text:
Saving your merry humor, here’s the note
How much your chain weighs to the utmost carat,
The fineness of the gold, and chargeful fashion,
Which doth amount to three-odd ducats more
Than I stand debted to this gentleman.
I pray you, see him presently discharged,
For he is bound to sea, and stays but for it.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
units.text:
I am not furnished with the present money.
Besides, I have some business in the town.
Good signior, take the stranger to my house,
And with you take the chain, and bid my wife
Disburse the sum on the receipt thereof.
Perchance I will be there as soon as you.

units.speaker: ANGELO
units.text: Then you will bring the chain to her yourself.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
units.text: No, bear it with you lest I come not time enough.

units.speaker: ANGELO
units.text: Well, sir, I will. Have you the chain about you?

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
units.text:
An if I have not, sir, I hope you have,
Or else you may return without your money.

units.speaker: ANGELO
units.text:
Nay, come, I pray you, sir, give me the chain.
Both wind and tide stays for this gentleman,
And I, to blame, have held him here too long.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
units.text:
Good Lord! You use this dalliance to excuse
Your breach of promise to the Porpentine.
I should have chid you for not bringing it,
But, like a shrew, you first begin to brawl.

units.speaker: SECOND MERCHANT
units.text: The hour steals on. I pray you, sir, dispatch.

units.speaker: ANGELO
units.text: You hear how he importunes me. The chain!

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
units.text: Why, give it to my wife, and fetch your money.

units.speaker: ANGELO
units.text:
Come, come. You know I gave it you even now.
Either send the chain, or send me by some token.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
units.text:
Fie, now you run this humor out of breath.
Come, where’s the chain? I pray you, let me see it.

units.speaker: SECOND MERCHANT
units.text:
My business cannot brook this dalliance.
[to ANTIPHOLUS] Good sir, say whe'er you’ll answer me
or no.
If not, I’ll leave him to the Officer.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
units.text: I answer you? What should I answer you?

units.speaker: ANGELO
units.text: The money that you owe me for the chain.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
units.text: I owe you none till I receive the chain.

units.speaker: ANGELO
units.text: You know I gave it you half an hour since.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
units.text: You gave me none. You wrong me much to say so.

units.speaker: ANGELO
units.text:
You wrong me more, sir, in denying it.
Consider how it stands upon my credit.

units.speaker: SECOND MERCHANT
units.text: Well, officer, arrest him at my suit.

units.speaker: OFFICER
units.text:
I do, [to ANGELO] and charge you in the Duke’s name to
obey me.

units.speaker: ANGELO
units.text:
This touches me in reputation.
Either consent to pay this sum for me,
Or I attach you by this officer.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
units.text:
Consent to pay thee that I never had?—
Arrest me, foolish fellow, if thou dar’st.

units.speaker: ANGELO
units.text:
Here is thy fee. Arrest him, officer.
I would not spare my brother in this case
If he should scorn me so apparently.

units.speaker: OFFICER
units.text: I do arrest you, sir. You hear the suit.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
units.text:
I do obey thee till I give thee bail.
But, sirrah, you shall buy this sport as dear
As all the metal in your shop will answer.

units.speaker: ANGELO
units.text:
Sir, sir, I will have law in Ephesus,
To your notorious shame, I doubt it not.
Enter DROMIO OF SYRACUSE

units.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
Master, there is a bark of Epidamnum
That stays but till her owner comes aboard,
And then, sir, she bears away. Our fraughtage, sir,
I have conveyed aboard, and I have bought
The oil, the balsamum and aqua vitae.
The ship is in her trim; the merry wind
Blows fair from land. They stay for naught at all
But for their owner, master, and yourself.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
units.text:
How now? A madman? Why, thou peevish sheep,
What ship of Epidamnum stays for me?

units.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
units.text: A ship you sent me to, to hire waftage.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
units.text:
Thou drunken slave, I sent thee for a rope
And told thee to what purpose and what end.

units.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
You sent me for a rope’s end as soon.
You sent me to the bay, sir, for a bark.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
units.text:
I will debate this matter at more leisure
And teach your ears to list me with more heed.
To Adriana, villain, hie thee straight.
Give her this key, and tell her in the desk
That’s cover’d o'er with Turkish tapestry
There is a purse of ducats. Let her send it.
Tell her I am arrested in the street,
And that shall bail me. Hie thee, slave. Begone.—
On, officer, to prison till it come.
Exeunt SECOND MERCHANT, ANGELO, OFFICER, and ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS

units.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
To Adriana. That is where we dined,
Where Dowsabel did claim me for her husband.
She is too big, I hope, for me to compass.
Thither I must, although against my will,
For servants must their masters' minds fulfill.
Exit
Enter ADRIANA and LUCIANA

units.speaker: ADRIANA
units.text:
Ah, Luciana, did he tempt thee so?
Mightst thou perceive austerely in his eye
That he did plead in earnest, yea or no?
Looked he or red or pale, or sad or merrily?
What observation mad’st thou in this case
Of his heart’s meteors tilting in his face?

units.speaker: LUCIANA
units.text: First he denied you had in him no right.

units.speaker: ADRIANA
units.text: He meant he did me none; the more my spite.

units.speaker: LUCIANA
units.text: Then swore he that he was a stranger here.

units.speaker: ADRIANA
units.text: And true he swore, though yet forsworn he were.

units.speaker: LUCIANA
units.text: Then pleaded I for you.

units.speaker: ADRIANA
units.text: And what said he?

units.speaker: LUCIANA
units.text: That love i begged for you he begged of me.

units.speaker: ADRIANA
units.text: With what persuasion did he tempt thy love?

units.speaker: LUCIANA
units.text:
With words that in an honest suit might move.
First he did praise my beauty, then my speech.

units.speaker: ADRIANA
units.text: Did’st speak him fair?

units.speaker: LUCIANA
units.text: Have patience, I beseech.

units.speaker: ADRIANA
units.text:
I cannot, nor I will not hold me still;
My tongue, though not my heart, shall have his will.
He is deformèd, crooked, old, and sere,
Ill-faced, worse-bodied, shapeless everywhere,
Vicious, ungentle, foolish, blunt, unkind,
Stigmatical in making, worse in mind.

units.speaker: LUCIANA
units.text:
Who would be jealous, then, of such a one?
No evil lost is wailed when it is gone.

units.speaker: ADRIANA
units.text:
Ah, but I think him better than I say,
And yet would herein others' eyes were worse.
Far from her nest the lapwing cries away.
My heart prays for him, though my tongue do curse.
Enter DROMIO OF SYRACUSE, running

units.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
units.text: Here, go—the desk, the purse! Sweet, now make haste.

units.speaker: LUCIANA
units.text: How hast thou lost thy breath?

units.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
units.text: By running fast.

units.speaker: ADRIANA
units.text: Where is thy master, Dromio? Is he well?

units.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
ADRIANA
units.text: Why, man, what is the matter?

units.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
units.text: I do not know the matter. He is ’rested on the case.

units.speaker: ADRIANA
units.text: What, is he arrested? Tell me at whose suit.

units.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
I know not at whose suit he is arrested well,
But he’s in a suit of buff which ’rested him; that can
I tell.
Will you send him, mistress, redemption—the money in
his
desk?

units.speaker: ADRIANA
units.text: Go fetch it, sister.
Exit LUCIANA

units.speaker: ADRIANA
units.text:
This I wonder at,
That he, unknown to me, should be in debt.
Tell me, was he arrested on a bond?

units.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
Not on a bond, but on a stronger thing:
A chain, a chain. Do you not hear it ring?

units.speaker: ADRIANA
units.text: What, the chain?

units.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
No, no, the bell. 'Tis time that I were gone.
It was two ere I left him, and now the clock strikes
one.

units.speaker: ADRIANA
units.text: The hours come back. That did I never hear.

units.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
O yes, if any hour meet a sergeant, he turns back for
very fear.

units.speaker: ADRIANA
units.text: As if time were in debt. How fondly dost thou reason!

units.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
Time is a very bankrupt and owes more than he’s worth
to
season.
Nay, he’s a thief too. Have you not heard men say
That time comes stealing on by night and day?
If he be in debt and theft, and a sergeant in the way,
Hath he not reason to turn back an hour in a day?
Re-enter LUCIANA with a purse

units.speaker: ADRIANA
units.text:
Go, Dromio. There’s the money. Bear it straight,
And bring thy master home immediately.
Come, sister, I am pressed down with conceit:
Conceit, my comfort and my injury.
Exeunt
Enter ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
There’s not a man I meet but doth salute me
As if I were their well-acquainted friend,
And every one doth call me by my name.
Some tender money to me; some invite me;
Some other give me thanks for kindnesses;
Some offer me commodities to buy.
Even now a tailor called me in his shop
And showed me silks that he had bought for me,
And therewithal took measure of my body.
Sure, these are but imaginary wiles,
And lapland sorcerers inhabit here.
Enter DROMIO OF SYRACUSE

units.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
Master, here’s The gold you sent me for. What, have you
got redemption of the picture of old Adam
new-appareled?

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
units.text: What gold is this? What Adam dost thou mean?

units.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
Not that Adam that kept the Paradise, but that Adam
that keeps the prison; he that goes in the calf’s skin
that was killed for the Prodigal; he that came behind
you, sir, like an evil angel, and bid you forsake your
liberty.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
units.text: I understand thee not.

units.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
No? Why, ’tis a plain case: he that went, like a bass
viol in a case of leather; the man, sir, that, when
gentlemen are tired, gives them a sob and ’rests them;
he, sir, that takes pity on decayed men and gives them
suits of durance; he that sets up his rest to do more
exploits with his mace than a morris-pike.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
units.text: What, thou meanest an officer?

units.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
Ay, sir, the sergeant of the band; he that brings any
man to answer it that breaks his bond; one that thinks a
man always going to bed and says “God give you good
rest.”

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
Well, sir, there rest in your foolery. Is there any
ships put forth tonight? May we be gone?

units.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
Why, sir, I brought you word an hour since that the
bark Expedition put forth tonight, and then were you
hindered by the sergeant to tarry for the hoy Delay .
Here are the angels that you sent for to deliver you.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
The fellow is distract, and so am I,
And here we wander in illusions.
Some blessed power deliver us from hence!
Enter a COURTESAN

units.speaker: COURTESAN
units.text:
Well met, well met, Master Antipholus.
I see, sir, you have found the goldsmith now.
Is that the chain you promised me today?

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
units.text: Satan, avoid! I charge thee, tempt me not.

units.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
units.text: Master, is this Mistress Satan?

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
units.text: It is the devil.

units.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
Nay, she is worse; she is the devil’s dam, and here she
comes in the habit of a light wench. And thereof comes
that the wenches say “God damn me” that’s as much to say
“God make me a light wench.” It is written they appear
to men like angels of light. Light is an effect of fire,
and fire will burn: ergo, light wenches will burn. Come
not near her.

units.speaker: COURTESAN
units.text:
Your man and you are marvelous merry, sir.
Will you go with me? We’ll mend our dinner here.

units.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
Master, if you do, expect spoon meat; or bespeak a long
spoon.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
units.text: Why, Dromio?

units.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
Marry, he must have a long spoon that must eat with the
devil.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
[to COURTESAN] Avoid then, fiend! What tell’st thou me
of supping?
Thou art, as you are all, a sorceress.
I conjure thee to leave me and be gone.

units.speaker: COURTESAN
units.text:
Give me the ring of mine you had at dinner
Or, for my diamond, the chain you promised,
And I’ll be gone, sir, and not trouble you.

units.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
Some devils ask but the parings of one’s nail, a rush,
a hair, a drop of blood, a pin, a nut, a cherrystone;
but she, more covetous, would have a chain. Master, be
wise. An if you give it her, the devil will shake her
chain and fright us with it.

units.speaker: COURTESAN
units.text:
I pray you, sir, my ring or else the chain.
I hope you do not mean to cheat me so.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
units.text: Avaunt, thou witch!—Come, Dromio, let us go.

units.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
units.text: “Fly pride,” says the peacock. Mistress, that you know.
Exeunt ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE and DROMIO OF SYRACUSE

units.speaker: COURTESAN
units.text:
Now, out of doubt Antipholus is mad;
Else would he never so demean himself.
A ring he hath of mine worth forty ducats,
And for the same he promised me a chain.
Both one and other he denies me now.
The reason that I gather he is mad,
Besides this present instance of his rage,
Is a mad tale he told today at dinner
Of his own doors being shut against his entrance.
Belike his wife, acquainted with his fits,
On purpose shut the doors against his way.
My way is now to hie home to his house
And tell his wife that, being lunatic,
He rushed into my house and took perforce
My ring away. This course I fittest choose,
For forty ducats is too much to lose.
Exit
Enter ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS and the OFFICER

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
units.text:
Fear me not, man. I will not break away:
I’ll give thee, ere I leave thee, so much money,
To warrant thee, as I am ’rested for.
My wife is in a wayward mood today
And will not lightly trust the messenger
That I should be attached in Ephesus.
I tell you, ’twill sound harshly in her ears.
Enter DROMIO OF EPHESUS with a rope’s end

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
units.text:
Here comes my man. I think he brings the money.
How now, sir? Have you that I sent you for?

units.speaker: DROMIO OF EPHESUS
units.text: Here’s that, I warrant you, will pay them all.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
units.text: But where’s the money?

units.speaker: DROMIO OF EPHESUS
units.text: Why, sir, I gave the money for the rope.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
units.text: Five hundred ducats, villain, for a rope?

units.speaker: DROMIO OF EPHESUS
units.text: I’ll serve you, sir, five hundred at the rate.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
units.text: To what end did I bid thee hie thee home?

units.speaker: DROMIO OF EPHESUS
units.text: To a rope’s end, sir, and to that end am I returned.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
units.text:
And to that end, sir, I will welcome you. [beats DROMIO
OF EPHESUS]

units.speaker: OFFICER
units.text: Good sir, be patient.

units.speaker: DROMIO OF EPHESUS
units.text: Nay, ’tis for me to be patient. I am in adversity.

units.speaker: OFFICER
units.text: Good now, hold thy tongue.

units.speaker: DROMIO OF EPHESUS
units.text: Nay, rather persuade him to hold his hands.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
units.text: Thou whoreson, senseless villain.

units.speaker: DROMIO OF EPHESUS
units.text:
I would I were senseless, sir, that I might not feel
your blows.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
units.text:
Thou art sensible in nothing but blows, and so is an
ass.

units.speaker: DROMIO OF EPHESUS
units.text:
I am an ass, indeed; you may prove it by my long
ears.—I have served him from the hour of my nativity to
this instant, and have nothing at his hands for my
service but blows. When I am cold, he heats me with
beating; when I am warm, he cools me with beating. I am
waked with it when I sleep, raised with it when I sit,
driven out of doors with it when I go from home,
welcomed home with it when I return. Nay, I bear it on
my shoulders as a beggar wont her brat, and I think when
he hath lamed me, I shall beg with it from door to
door.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
units.text: Come, go along. My wife is coming yonder.
Enter ADRIANA, LUCIANA, the COURTESAN and a schoolmaster called PINCH

units.speaker: DROMIO OF EPHESUS
units.text:
Mistress, respice finem, respect your end, or rather,
the prophecy like the parrot, “Beware the rope’s end.”

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
units.text: Wilt thou still talk? [beats DROMIO OF EPHESUS]

units.speaker: COURTESAN
units.text: How say you now? Is not your husband mad?

units.speaker: ADRIANA
units.text:
His incivility confirms no less.—
Good Doctor Pinch, you are a conjurer;
Establish him in his true sense again,
And I will please you what you will demand.

units.speaker: LUCIANA
units.text: Alas, how fiery and how sharp he looks!

units.speaker: COURTESAN
units.text: Mark how he trembles in his ecstasy.

units.speaker: PINCH
units.text: Give me your hand, and let me feel your pulse.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
units.text:
There is my hand, and let it feel your ear. [strikes
PINCH]

units.speaker: PINCH
units.text:
I charge thee, Satan, housed within this man,
To yield possession to my holy prayers
And to thy state of darkness hie thee straight.
I conjure thee by all the saints in heaven.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
units.text: Peace, doting wizard, peace. I am not mad.

units.speaker: ADRIANA
units.text: O, that thou wert not, poor distressèd soul!

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
units.text:
You minion, you, are these your customers?
Did this companion with the saffron face
Revel and feast it at my house today
Whilst upon me the guilty doors were shut
And I denied to enter in my house?

units.speaker: ADRIANA
units.text:
O husband, God doth know you dined at home,
Where would you had remained until this time,
Free from these slanders and this open shame.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
units.text: “Dined at home”? Thou villain, what sayest thou?

units.speaker: DROMIO OF EPHESUS
units.text: Sir, sooth to say, you did not dine at home.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
units.text: Were not my doors locked up and I shut out?

units.speaker: DROMIO OF EPHESUS
units.text: Perdie, your doors were locked, and you shut out.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
units.text: And did not she herself revile me there?

units.speaker: DROMIO OF EPHESUS
units.text: Sans fable, she herself reviled you there.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
units.text: Did not her kitchen maid rail, taunt, and scorn me?

units.speaker: DROMIO OF EPHESUS
units.text: Certes, she did; the kitchen vestal scorned you.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
units.text: And did not I in rage depart from thence?

units.speaker: DROMIO OF EPHESUS
units.text:
In verity you did.—My bones bear witness,
That since have felt the vigour of his rage.

units.speaker: ADRIANA
units.text: [To PINCH] Is’t good to soothe him in these contraries?

units.speaker: PINCH
units.text:
It is no shame. The fellow finds his vein
And, yielding to him, humors well his frenzy.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
units.text:
[to ADRIANA ] Thou hast suborned the goldsmith to arrest
me.

units.speaker: ADRIANA
units.text:
Alas, I sent you money to redeem you
By Dromio here, who came in haste for it.

units.speaker: DROMIO OF EPHESUS
units.text:
Money by me! heart and goodwill you might,
But surely, master, not a rag of money.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
units.text: Went’st not thou to her for a purse of ducats?

units.speaker: ADRIANA
units.text: He came to me, and I delivered it.

units.speaker: LUCIANA
units.text: And I am witness with her that she did.

units.speaker: DROMIO OF EPHESUS
units.text:
God and the rope-maker bear me witness
That I was sent for nothing but a rope.

units.speaker: PINCH
units.text:
Mistress, both man and master is possessed.
I know it by their pale and deadly looks.
They must be bound and laid in some dark room.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
units.text:
[to ADRIANA ] Say wherefore didst thou lock me forth
today.
[to DROMIO OF EPHESUS ] And why dost thou deny the bag
of gold?

units.speaker: ADRIANA
units.text: I did not, gentle husband, lock thee forth.

units.speaker: DROMIO OF EPHESUS
units.text:
And, gentle master, I received no gold.
But I confess, sir, that we were locked out.

units.speaker: ADRIANA
units.text: Dissembling villain, thou speak’st false in both.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
units.text:
Dissembling harlot, thou art false in all,
And art confederate with a damnèd pack
To make a loathsome abject scorn of me.
But with these nails I’ll pluck out these false eyes
That would behold in me this shameful sport.
Enter three or four, and offer to bind him. He strives.

units.speaker: ADRIANA
units.text: O bind him, bind him! Let him not come near me.

units.speaker: PINCH
units.text: More company! The fiend is strong within him.

units.speaker: LUCIANA
units.text: Ay me, poor man, how pale and wan he looks!

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
units.text:
What, will you murder me?—Thou jailer, thou,
I am thy prisoner. Wilt thou suffer them
To make a rescue?

units.speaker: OFFICER
units.text:
Masters, let him go.
He is my prisoner, and you shall not have him.

units.speaker: PINCH
units.text: Go, bind this man, for he is frantic too.

units.speaker: ADRIANA
units.text:
What wilt thou do, thou peevish officer?
Hast thou delight to see a wretched man
Do outrage and displeasure to himself?

units.speaker: OFFICER
units.text:
He is my prisoner. If I let him go,
The debt he owes will be required of me.

units.speaker: ADRIANA
units.text:
I will discharge thee ere I go from thee.
Bear me forthwith unto his creditor,
And, knowing how the debt grows, I will pay it.—
Good Master Doctor, see him safe conveyed
Home to my house. O most unhappy day!

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
units.text: O most unhappy strumpet!

units.speaker: DROMIO OF EPHESUS
units.text: Master, I am here entered in bond for you.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
units.text: Out on thee, villain! Wherefore dost thou mad me?

units.speaker: DROMIO OF EPHESUS
units.text:
Will you be bound for nothing? Be mad, good master.
Cry “The devil!”

units.speaker: LUCIANA
units.text: God help poor souls! How idly do they talk!

units.speaker: ADRIANA
units.text: Go bear him hence. Sister, go you with me.
Exeunt PINCH and the men, with ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS and DROMIO OF EPHESUS

units.speaker: ADRIANA
units.text: Manent OFFICER, ADRIANA, LUCIANA, COURTESAN

units.speaker: OFFICER
units.text: One Angelo, a goldsmith. Do you know him?

units.speaker: ADRIANA
units.text: I know the man. What is the sum he owes?

units.speaker: OFFICER
units.text: Two hundred ducats.

units.speaker: ADRIANA
units.text: Say, how grows it due?

units.speaker: OFFICER
units.text: Due for a chain your husband had of him.

units.speaker: ADRIANA
units.text: He did bespeak a chain for me but had it not.

units.speaker: COURTESAN
units.text:
Whenas your husband all in rage today
Came to my house and took away my ring,
The ring I saw upon his finger now,
Straight after did I meet him with a chain.

units.speaker: ADRIANA
units.text:
It may be so, but I did never see it.—
Come, jailer, bring me where the goldsmith is.
I long to know the truth hereof at large.
Enter ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE with his rapier drawn and DROMIO OF SYRACUSE

units.speaker: LUCIANA
units.text: God for Thy mercy, they are loose again!

units.speaker: ADRIANA
units.text:
And come with naked swords. Let’s call more help
To have them bound again.

units.speaker: OFFICER
units.text: Away! They’ll kill us.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
units.text: I see these witches are afraid of swords.

units.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
units.text: She that would be your wife now ran from you.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
Come to the Centaur. Fetch our stuff from thence.
I long that we were safe and sound aboard.

units.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
Faith, stay here this night. They will surely do us no
harm. You saw they speak us fair, give us gold. Methinks
they are such a gentle nation that, but for the
mountain of mad flesh that claims marriage of me, i
could find in my heart to stay here still, and turn
witch.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
I will not stay tonight for all the town.
Therefore away, to get our stuff aboard.
Exeunt
Enter SECOND MERCHANT and ANGELO the goldsmith

units.speaker: ANGELO
units.text:
I am sorry, sir, that I have hindered you,
But I protest he had the chain of me,
Though most dishonestly he doth deny it.

units.speaker: SECOND MERCHANT
units.text: How is the man esteemed here in the city?

units.speaker: ANGELO
units.text:
Of very reverend reputation, sir,
Of credit infinite, highly beloved,
Second to none that lives here in the city.
His word might bear my wealth at any time.

units.speaker: SECOND MERCHANT
units.text: Speak softly. Yonder, as I think, he walks.
ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE and DROMIO OF SYRACUSE again

units.speaker: ANGELO
units.text:
'Tis so; and that self chain about his neck
Which he forswore most monstrously to have.
Good sir, draw near to me. I’ll speak to him.—
Signior Antipholus, I wonder much
That you would put me to this shame and trouble,
And not without some scandal to yourself,
With circumstance and oaths so to deny
This chain, which now you wear so openly.
Beside the charge, the shame, imprisonment,
You have done wrong to this my honest friend,
Who, but for staying on our controversy,
Had hoisted sail and put to sea today.
This chain you had of me. Can you deny it?

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
units.text: I think I had. I never did deny it.

units.speaker: SECOND MERCHANT
units.text: Yes, that you did, sir, and forswore it too.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
units.text: Who heard me to deny it or forswear it?

units.speaker: SECOND MERCHANT
units.text:
These ears of mine, thou know’st did hear thee.
Fie on thee, wretch. 'Tis pity that thou liv’st
To walk where any honest men resort.

units.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
units.text:
Thou art a villain to impeach me thus.
I’ll prove mine honor and mine honesty
Against thee presently if thou dar’st stand.

units.speaker: SECOND MERCHANT
units.text: I dare, and do defy thee for a villain.
They draw Enter ADRIANA, LUCIANA, the COURTESAN and others

units.speaker: ADRIANA
units.text:
