Look sweet, be fair, become disloyalty;
Apparel vice like virtue’s harbinger.
Bear a fair presence, though your heart be tainted.
Teach sin the carriage of a holy saint.
Be secret-false. What need she be acquainted?
What simple thief brags of his own attaint?
'Tis double wrong to truant with your bed
And let her read it in thy looks at board.
Shame hath a bastard fame, well managèd;
Ill deeds is doubled with an evil word.
Alas, poor women, make us but believe,
Being compact of credit, that you love us.
Though others have the arm, show us the sleeve;
We in your motion turn, and you may move us.
Then, gentle brother, get you in again.
Comfort my sister, cheer her, call her wife.
'Tis holy sport to be a little vain
When the sweet breath of flattery conquers strife.

sentences.speaker: LUCIANA
sentences.text:
And may it be that you have quite forgot
A husband’s office? Shall, Antipholus,
Even in the spring of love thy love-springs rot?
Shall love, in building, grow so ruinous?
If you did wed my sister for her wealth,
Then for her wealth’s sake use her with more kindness.
Or if you like elsewhere, do it by stealth—
Muffle your false love with some show of blindness.
Let not my sister read it in your eye;
Be not thy tongue thy own shame’s orator;
Look sweet, be fair, become disloyalty;
Apparel vice like virtue’s harbinger.
Bear a fair presence, though your heart be tainted.
Teach sin the carriage of a holy saint.
Be secret-false. What need she be acquainted?
What simple thief brags of his own attaint?
'Tis double wrong to truant with your bed
And let her read it in thy looks at board.
Shame hath a bastard fame, well managèd;
Ill deeds is doubled with an evil word.
Alas, poor women, make us but believe,
Being compact of credit, that you love us.
Though others have the arm, show us the sleeve;
We in your motion turn, and you may move us.
Then, gentle brother, get you in again.
Comfort my sister, cheer her, call her wife.
'Tis holy sport to be a little vain
When the sweet breath of flattery conquers strife.

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text:
Sweet mistress—what your name is else I know not,
Nor by what wonder you do hit of mine,—
Less in your knowledge and your grace you show not
Than our earth’s wonder, more than earth divine.
Teach me, dear creature, how to think and speak.
Lay open to my earthy gross conceit,
Smothered in errors, feeble, shallow, weak,
The folded meaning of your words' deceit.
Against my soul’s pure truth why labour you
To make it wander in an unknown field?
Are you a god? would you create me new?
Transform me, then, and to your power I’ll yield.
But if that I am I, then well I know
Your weeping sister is no wife of mine,
Nor to her bed no homage do I owe.
Far more, far more, to you do I decline.
O, train me not, sweet mermaid, with thy note
To drown me in thy sister’s flood of tears.
Sing, Siren, for thyself, and I will dote.
Spread o'er the silver waves thy golden hairs,
And as a bed I’ll take them and there lie,
And in that glorious supposition think
He gains by death that hath such means to die.
Let Love, being light, be drownèd if she sink.

sentences.speaker: LUCIANA
sentences.text: What, are you mad that you do reason so?

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text: Not mad, but mated—how, I do not know.

sentences.speaker: LUCIANA
sentences.text: It is a fault that springeth from your eye.

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text: For gazing on your beams, fair sun, being by.

sentences.speaker: LUCIANA
sentences.text: Gaze where you should, and that will clear your sight.

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text: As good to wink, sweet love, as look on night.

sentences.speaker: LUCIANA
sentences.text: Why call you me “love”? Call my sister so.

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text: Thy sister’s sister.

sentences.speaker: LUCIANA
sentences.text: That’s my sister.

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text:
No,
It is thyself, mine own self’s better part,
Mine eye’s clear eye, my dear heart’s dearer heart,
My food, my fortune, and my sweet hope’s aim,
My sole earth’s heaven, and my heaven’s claim.

sentences.speaker: LUCIANA
sentences.text: All this my sister is, or else should be.

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text:
Call thyself “sister,” sweet, for I am thee.
Thee will I love and with thee lead my life;
Thou hast no husband yet, nor I no wife.
Give me thy hand.

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: LUCIANA

O soft, sir! Hold you still.
I’ll fetch my sister to get her goodwill.

Enter DROMIO OF SYRACUSE

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text: Why, how now, Dromio. Where runn’st thou so fast?

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text: Why, how now, Dromio. Where runn’st thou so fast?

sentences.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text:
Do you know me, sir? Am I Dromio? Am I your man? Am
I myself?

sentences.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text:
Do you know me, sir? Am I Dromio? Am I your man? Am
I myself?

sentences.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text:
Do you know me, sir? Am I Dromio? Am I your man? Am
I myself?

sentences.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text:
Do you know me, sir? Am I Dromio? Am I your man? Am
I myself?

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text: Thou art Dromio, thou art my man, thou art thyself.

sentences.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text: I am an ass, I am a woman’s man, and besides myself.

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text: What woman’s man? And how besides thyself?

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text: What woman’s man? And how besides thyself?

sentences.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text:
Marry, sir, besides myself I am due to a woman, one
that claims me, one that haunts me, one that will have
me.

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text: What claim lays she to thee?

sentences.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text:
Marry, sir, such claim as you would lay to your horse;
and she would have me as a beast; not that I being a
beast she would have me, but that she, being a very
beastly creature, lays claim to me.

sentences.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text:
Marry, sir, such claim as you would lay to your horse;
and she would have me as a beast; not that I being a
beast she would have me, but that she, being a very
beastly creature, lays claim to me.

sentences.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text:
Marry, sir, such claim as you would lay to your horse;
and she would have me as a beast; not that I being a
beast she would have me, but that she, being a very
beastly creature, lays claim to me.

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text: What is she?

sentences.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text:
A very reverent body, ay, such a one as a man may not
speak of without he say “sir-reverence.” I have but lean
luck in the match, and yet is she a wondrous fat
marriage.

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text: How dost thou mean a “fat marriage”?

sentences.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text:
Marry, sir, she’s the kitchen wench, and all grease,
and I know not what use to put her to but to make a lamp
of her and run from her by her own light. I warrant her
rags and the tallow in them will burn a Poland winter.
If she lives till doomsday, she’ll burn a week longer
than the whole world.

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text: What complexion is she of?

sentences.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text:
Swart like my shoe, but her face nothing like so clean
kept. For why? She sweats a man may go overshoes in the
grime of it.

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text: That’s a fault that water will mend.

sentences.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text: No, sir, ’tis in grain; Noah’s flood could not do it.

sentences.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text: No, sir, ’tis in grain; Noah’s flood could not do it.

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text: What’s her name?

sentences.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text:
Nell, sir, but her name and three quarters—that’s an
ell and three quarters—will not measure her from hip to
hip.

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text: Then she bears some breadth?

sentences.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text:
No longer from head to foot than from hip to hip. She
is spherical, like a globe. I could find out countries
in her.

sentences.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text:
No longer from head to foot than from hip to hip. She
is spherical, like a globe. I could find out countries
in her.

sentences.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE

is spherical, like a globe. I could find out countries
in her.

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text: In what part of her body stands Ireland?

sentences.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text:
Marry, sir, in her buttocks. I found it out by the
bogs.

sentences.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text:
Marry, sir, in her buttocks. I found it out by the
bogs.

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text: Where Scotland?

sentences.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text:
I found it by the barrenness; hard in the palm of the
hand.

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text: Where France?

sentences.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text:
In her forehead, armed and reverted, making war against
her heir.

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text: Where England?

sentences.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text:
I looked for the chalky cliffs, but I could find no
whiteness in them. But I guess it stood in her chin, by
the salt rheum that ran between France and it.

sentences.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text:
I looked for the chalky cliffs, but I could find no
whiteness in them. But I guess it stood in her chin, by
the salt rheum that ran between France and it.

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text: Where Spain?

sentences.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text: Faith, I saw it not, but I felt it hot in her breath.

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text: Where America, the Indies?

sentences.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text:
O, sir, upon her nose, all o'er-embellished with
rubies, carbuncles, sapphires, declining their rich
aspect to the hot breath of Spain, who sent whole
armadas of caracks to be ballast at her nose.

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text: Where stood Belgia, the Netherlands?

sentences.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text:
O, sir, I did not look so low. To conclude: this drudge
or diviner laid claim to me, call’d me Dromio, swore I
was assured to her, told me what privy marks I had about
me, as the mark of my shoulder, the mole in my neck,
the great wart on my left arm, that I, amazed, ran from
her as a witch. And, I think, if my breast had not been
made of faith, and my heart of steel, She had
transformed me to a curtal dog and made me turn i' th'
wheel.

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text:
Go, hie thee presently. Post to the road.
An if the wind blow any way from shore,
I will not harbor in this town tonight.
If any bark put forth, come to the mart,
Where I will walk till thou return to me.
If every one knows us, and we know none,
'Tis time, I think, to trudge, pack, and be gone.

sentences.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text:
As from a bear a man would run for life,
So fly I from her that would be my wife.
Exit DROMIO OF SYRACUSE

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text:
There’s none but witches do inhabit here,
And therefore ’tis high time that I were hence.
She that doth call me husband, even my soul
Doth for a wife abhor. But her fair sister,
Possessed with such a gentle sovereign grace,
Of such enchanting presence and discourse,
Hath almost made me traitor to myself.
But lest myself be guilty to self wrong,
I’ll stop mine ears against the mermaid’s song.

Enter ANGELO with the chain

sentences.speaker: ANGELO
sentences.text: Master Antipholus.

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text: Ay, that’s my name.

sentences.speaker: ANGELO
ANGELO
ANGELO
ANGELO
ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text: What is your will that I shall do with this?

sentences.speaker: ANGELO
sentences.text: What please yourself, sir. I have made it for you.

sentences.speaker: ANGELO
sentences.text: What please yourself, sir. I have made it for you.

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text: Made it for me, sir? I bespoke it not.

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text: Made it for me, sir? I bespoke it not.

sentences.speaker: ANGELO
sentences.text:
Not once, nor twice, but twenty times you have.
Go home with it and please your wife withal,
And soon at supper time I’ll visit you
And then receive my money for the chain.

sentences.speaker: ANGELO
sentences.text:
Not once, nor twice, but twenty times you have.
Go home with it and please your wife withal,
And soon at supper time I’ll visit you
And then receive my money for the chain.

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text:
I pray you, sir, receive the money now,
For fear you ne'er see chain nor money more.

sentences.speaker: ANGELO
sentences.text: You are a merry man, sir. Fare you well.

sentences.speaker: ANGELO
sentences.text: You are a merry man, sir. Fare you well.
Exit ANGELO

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text:
What I should think of this I cannot tell,
But this I think: there’s no man is so vain
That would refuse so fair an offered chain.
I see a man here needs not live by shifts
When in the streets he meets such golden gifts.
I’ll to the mart, and there for Dromio stay.
If any ship put out, then straight away.

Enter SECOND MERCHANT, ANGELO and an OFFICER

sentences.speaker: SECOND MERCHANT
sentences.text:
You know since Pentecost the sum is due,
And since I have not much importuned you,
Nor now I had not, but that I am bound
To Persia, and want guilders for my voyage.
Therefore make present satisfaction,
Or I’ll attach you by this officer.

sentences.speaker: SECOND MERCHANT

Therefore make present satisfaction,
Or I’ll attach you by this officer.

sentences.speaker: SECOND MERCHANT

sentences.speaker: ANGELO
sentences.text:
Even just the sum that I do owe to you
Is growing to me by Antipholus.
And in the instant that I met with you,
He had of me a chain. At five o'clock
I shall receive the money for the same.
Pleaseth you walk with me down to his house,
I will discharge my bond and thank you too.

Enter ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS and DROMIO OF EPHESUS from the COURTESAN'S house

sentences.speaker: COURTESAN S OFFICER
sentences.text: That labor may you save. See where he comes.

sentences.speaker: COURTESAN S OFFICER
sentences.text: That labor may you save. See where he comes.

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
sentences.text:
While I go to the goldsmith’s house, go thou
And buy a rope’s end. That will I bestow
Among my wife and her confederates
For locking me out of my doors by day.
But soft. I see the goldsmith. Get thee gone.
Buy thou a rope, and bring it home to me.

sentences.speaker: DROMIO OF EPHESUS
sentences.text: I buy a thousand pound a year! I buy a rope!
Exit DROMIO OF EPHESUS

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
sentences.text:
[to ANGELO] A man is well holp up that trusts to you!
I promisèd your presence and the chain,
But neither chain nor goldsmith came to me.
Belike you thought our love would last too long
If it were chained together, and therefore came not.

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: ANGELO
sentences.text:
Saving your merry humor, here’s the note
How much your chain weighs to the utmost carat,
The fineness of the gold, and chargeful fashion,
Which doth amount to three-odd ducats more
Than I stand debted to this gentleman.
I pray you, see him presently discharged,
For he is bound to sea, and stays but for it.

sentences.speaker: ANGELO

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
sentences.text:
I am not furnished with the present money.
Besides, I have some business in the town.
Good signior, take the stranger to my house,
And with you take the chain, and bid my wife
Disburse the sum on the receipt thereof.
Perchance I will be there as soon as you.

sentences.speaker: ANGELO
sentences.text: Then you will bring the chain to her yourself.

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
sentences.text: No, bear it with you lest I come not time enough.

sentences.speaker: ANGELO
sentences.text: Well, sir, I will. Have you the chain about you?

sentences.speaker: ANGELO
sentences.text: Well, sir, I will. Have you the chain about you?

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
sentences.text:
An if I have not, sir, I hope you have,
Or else you may return without your money.

sentences.speaker: ANGELO
sentences.text:
Nay, come, I pray you, sir, give me the chain.
Both wind and tide stays for this gentleman,
And I, to blame, have held him here too long.

sentences.speaker: ANGELO
sentences.text:
Nay, come, I pray you, sir, give me the chain.
Both wind and tide stays for this gentleman,
And I, to blame, have held him here too long.

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
sentences.text:
Good Lord! You use this dalliance to excuse
Your breach of promise to the Porpentine.
I should have chid you for not bringing it,
But, like a shrew, you first begin to brawl.

sentences.speaker: SECOND MERCHANT
sentences.text: The hour steals on. I pray you, sir, dispatch.

sentences.speaker: SECOND MERCHANT
sentences.text: The hour steals on. I pray you, sir, dispatch.

sentences.speaker: ANGELO
sentences.text: You hear how he importunes me. The chain!

sentences.speaker: ANGELO
sentences.text: You hear how he importunes me. The chain!

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
sentences.text: Why, give it to my wife, and fetch your money.

sentences.speaker: ANGELO
sentences.text:
Come, come. You know I gave it you even now.
Either send the chain, or send me by some token.

sentences.speaker: ANGELO
sentences.text:

Either send the chain, or send me by some token.

Either send the chain, or send me by some token.

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
sentences.text:
Fie, now you run this humor out of breath.
Come, where’s the chain? I pray you, let me see it.

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
sentences.text:

Come, where’s the chain? I pray you, let me see it.

sentences.speaker: SECOND MERCHANT
sentences.text:
My business cannot brook this dalliance.
[to ANTIPHOLUS] Good sir, say whe'er you’ll answer me
or no.
If not, I’ll leave him to the Officer.

sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
sentences.text: I answer you? What should I answer you?

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
sentences.text: I answer you? What should I answer you?

sentences.speaker: ANGELO
sentences.text: The money that you owe me for the chain.

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
sentences.text: I owe you none till I receive the chain.

sentences.speaker: ANGELO
sentences.text: You know I gave it you half an hour since.

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
sentences.text: You gave me none. You wrong me much to say so.

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
sentences.text: You gave me none. You wrong me much to say so.

sentences.speaker: ANGELO
sentences.text:
You wrong me more, sir, in denying it.
Consider how it stands upon my credit.

sentences.speaker: ANGELO
sentences.text:

Consider how it stands upon my credit.

sentences.speaker: SECOND MERCHANT
sentences.text: Well, officer, arrest him at my suit.

sentences.speaker: OFFICER
sentences.text:
I do, [to ANGELO] and charge you in the Duke’s name to
obey me.

sentences.speaker: ANGELO
sentences.text:
This touches me in reputation.
Either consent to pay this sum for me,
Or I attach you by this officer.

sentences.speaker: ANGELO
sentences.text:
This touches me in reputation.
Either consent to pay this sum for me,
Or I attach you by this officer.

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
sentences.text:
Consent to pay thee that I never had?—
Arrest me, foolish fellow, if thou dar’st.

sentences.speaker: ANGELO
sentences.text:
Here is thy fee. Arrest him, officer.
I would not spare my brother in this case
If he should scorn me so apparently.

sentences.speaker: ANGELO
sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: ANGELO

sentences.speaker: OFFICER
sentences.text: I do arrest you, sir. You hear the suit.

sentences.speaker: OFFICER
sentences.text: I do arrest you, sir. You hear the suit.

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
sentences.text:
I do obey thee till I give thee bail.
But, sirrah, you shall buy this sport as dear
As all the metal in your shop will answer.

sentences.speaker: ANGELO
sentences.text:
Sir, sir, I will have law in Ephesus,
To your notorious shame, I doubt it not.
Enter DROMIO OF SYRACUSE

sentences.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text:
Master, there is a bark of Epidamnum
That stays but till her owner comes aboard,
And then, sir, she bears away. Our fraughtage, sir,
I have conveyed aboard, and I have bought
The oil, the balsamum and aqua vitae.
The ship is in her trim; the merry wind
Blows fair from land. They stay for naught at all
But for their owner, master, and yourself.

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
sentences.text:
How now? A madman? Why, thou peevish sheep,
What ship of Epidamnum stays for me?

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS

How now? A madman? Why, thou peevish sheep,
What ship of Epidamnum stays for me?

sentences.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text: A ship you sent me to, to hire waftage.

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
sentences.text:
Thou drunken slave, I sent thee for a rope
And told thee to what purpose and what end.

sentences.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text:
You sent me for a rope’s end as soon.
You sent me to the bay, sir, for a bark.

sentences.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text:

You sent me to the bay, sir, for a bark.

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
sentences.text:
I will debate this matter at more leisure
And teach your ears to list me with more heed.
To Adriana, villain, hie thee straight.
Give her this key, and tell her in the desk
That’s cover’d o'er with Turkish tapestry
There is a purse of ducats. Let her send it.
Tell her I am arrested in the street,
And that shall bail me. Hie thee, slave. Begone.—
On, officer, to prison till it come.

Where Dowsabel did claim me for her husband.
She is too big, I hope, for me to compass.
Thither I must, although against my will,
For servants must their masters' minds fulfill.

Enter ADRIANA and LUCIANA

sentences.speaker: ADRIANA
sentences.text:
Ah, Luciana, did he tempt thee so?
Mightst thou perceive austerely in his eye
That he did plead in earnest, yea or no?
Looked he or red or pale, or sad or merrily?
What observation mad’st thou in this case
Of his heart’s meteors tilting in his face?

sentences.speaker: LUCIANA
sentences.text: First he denied you had in him no right.

sentences.speaker: ADRIANA
sentences.text: He meant he did me none; the more my spite.

sentences.speaker: LUCIANA
sentences.text: Then swore he that he was a stranger here.

sentences.speaker: ADRIANA
sentences.text: And true he swore, though yet forsworn he were.

sentences.speaker: LUCIANA
sentences.text: Then pleaded I for you.

sentences.speaker: ADRIANA
sentences.text: And what said he?

sentences.speaker: LUCIANA
sentences.text: That love i begged for you he begged of me.

sentences.speaker: ADRIANA
sentences.text: With what persuasion did he tempt thy love?

sentences.speaker: LUCIANA
sentences.text:
With words that in an honest suit might move.
First he did praise my beauty, then my speech.

sentences.speaker: LUCIANA
sentences.text:
With words that in an honest suit might move.
First he did praise my beauty, then my speech.

sentences.speaker: ADRIANA
sentences.text: Did’st speak him fair?

sentences.speaker: LUCIANA
sentences.text: Have patience, I beseech.

sentences.speaker: ADRIANA
sentences.text:
I cannot, nor I will not hold me still;
My tongue, though not my heart, shall have his will.
He is deformèd, crooked, old, and sere,
Ill-faced, worse-bodied, shapeless everywhere,
Vicious, ungentle, foolish, blunt, unkind,
Stigmatical in making, worse in mind.

sentences.speaker: LUCIANA

Who would be jealous, then, of such a one?
No evil lost is wailed when it is gone.

sentences.speaker: LUCIANA

sentences.speaker: ADRIANA

Ah, but I think him better than I say,
And yet would herein others' eyes were worse.
Far from her nest the lapwing cries away.
My heart prays for him, though my tongue do curse.

Tell me, was he arrested on a bond?

A chain, a chain. Do you not hear it ring?

sentences.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE

O yes, if any hour meet a sergeant, he turns back for
very fear.

sentences.speaker: ADRIANA
sentences.text: As if time were in debt. How fondly dost thou reason!

sentences.speaker: ADRIANA
sentences.text: As if time were in debt. How fondly dost thou reason!

sentences.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text:
Time is a very bankrupt and owes more than he’s worth
to
season.
Nay, he’s a thief too. Have you not heard men say
That time comes stealing on by night and day?
If he be in debt and theft, and a sergeant in the way,
Hath he not reason to turn back an hour in a day?

Enter ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text:
There’s not a man I meet but doth salute me
As if I were their well-acquainted friend,
And every one doth call me by my name.
Some tender money to me; some invite me;
Some other give me thanks for kindnesses;
Some offer me commodities to buy.
Even now a tailor called me in his shop
And showed me silks that he had bought for me,
And therewithal took measure of my body.
Sure, these are but imaginary wiles,
And lapland sorcerers inhabit here.

Master, here’s The gold you sent me for. What, have you
got redemption of the picture of old Adam
new-appareled?

sentences.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text: What gold is this? What Adam dost thou mean?

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text: What gold is this? What Adam dost thou mean?

sentences.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text:
Not that Adam that kept the Paradise, but that Adam
that keeps the prison; he that goes in the calf’s skin
that was killed for the Prodigal; he that came behind
you, sir, like an evil angel, and bid you forsake your
liberty.

sentences.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE

sentences.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE

No? Why, ’tis a plain case: he that went, like a bass
viol in a case of leather; the man, sir, that, when
gentlemen are tired, gives them a sob and ’rests them;
he, sir, that takes pity on decayed men and gives them
suits of durance; he that sets up his rest to do more
exploits with his mace than a morris-pike.

sentences.text: Master, is this Mistress Satan?

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text: It is the devil.

sentences.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text:
Nay, she is worse; she is the devil’s dam, and here she
comes in the habit of a light wench. And thereof comes
that the wenches say “God damn me” that’s as much to say
“God make me a light wench.” It is written they appear
to men like angels of light. Light is an effect of fire,
and fire will burn: ergo, light wenches will burn. Come
not near her.

sentences.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text:

and fire will burn: ergo, light wenches will burn. Come

sentences.speaker: COURTESAN
sentences.text:
Your man and you are marvelous merry, sir.
Will you go with me? We’ll mend our dinner here.

sentences.speaker: COURTESAN
sentences.text:
Your man and you are marvelous merry, sir.
Will you go with me? We’ll mend our dinner here.

sentences.speaker: COURTESAN
sentences.text:
Your man and you are marvelous merry, sir.
Will you go with me? We’ll mend our dinner here.

sentences.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text:
Master, if you do, expect spoon meat; or bespeak a long
spoon.

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text: Why, Dromio?

sentences.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text:
Marry, he must have a long spoon that must eat with the
devil.

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text:
[to COURTESAN] Avoid then, fiend! What tell’st thou me
of supping?
Thou art, as you are all, a sorceress.
I conjure thee to leave me and be gone.

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text:
[to COURTESAN] Avoid then, fiend! What tell’st thou me
of supping?
Thou art, as you are all, a sorceress.
I conjure thee to leave me and be gone.

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text:
[to COURTESAN] Avoid then, fiend! What tell’st thou me
of supping?
Thou art, as you are all, a sorceress.
I conjure thee to leave me and be gone.

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text:
[to COURTESAN] Avoid then, fiend! What tell’st thou me
of supping?
Thou art, as you are all, a sorceress.
I conjure thee to leave me and be gone.

sentences.speaker: COURTESAN
sentences.text:
Give me the ring of mine you had at dinner
Or, for my diamond, the chain you promised,
And I’ll be gone, sir, and not trouble you.

sentences.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text:
Some devils ask but the parings of one’s nail, a rush,
a hair, a drop of blood, a pin, a nut, a cherrystone;
but she, more covetous, would have a chain. Master, be
wise. An if you give it her, the devil will shake her
chain and fright us with it.

sentences.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text:
Some devils ask but the parings of one’s nail, a rush,
a hair, a drop of blood, a pin, a nut, a cherrystone;
but she, more covetous, would have a chain. Master, be
wise. An if you give it her, the devil will shake her
chain and fright us with it.

sentences.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text:
Some devils ask but the parings of one’s nail, a rush,

sentences.speaker: COURTESAN
sentences.text:
I pray you, sir, my ring or else the chain.
I hope you do not mean to cheat me so.

sentences.speaker: COURTESAN
sentences.text:
I pray you, sir, my ring or else the chain.
I hope you do not mean to cheat me so.

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text: Avaunt, thou witch!—Come, Dromio, let us go.

sentences.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text: “Fly pride,” says the peacock. Mistress, that you know.

sentences.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text: “Fly pride,” says the peacock. Mistress, that you know.
Exeunt ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE and DROMIO OF SYRACUSE

sentences.speaker: COURTESAN
sentences.text:
Now, out of doubt Antipholus is mad;
Else would he never so demean himself.
A ring he hath of mine worth forty ducats,
And for the same he promised me a chain.
Both one and other he denies me now.
The reason that I gather he is mad,
Besides this present instance of his rage,
Is a mad tale he told today at dinner
Of his own doors being shut against his entrance.
Belike his wife, acquainted with his fits,
On purpose shut the doors against his way.
My way is now to hie home to his house
And tell his wife that, being lunatic,
He rushed into my house and took perforce
My ring away. This course I fittest choose,
For forty ducats is too much to lose.

Enter ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS and the OFFICER

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
sentences.text:
Fear me not, man. I will not break away:
I’ll give thee, ere I leave thee, so much money,
To warrant thee, as I am ’rested for.
My wife is in a wayward mood today
And will not lightly trust the messenger
That I should be attached in Ephesus.
I tell you, ’twill sound harshly in her ears.

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
sentences.text:

I’ll give thee, ere I leave thee, so much money,
To warrant thee, as I am ’rested for.
My wife is in a wayward mood today
And will not lightly trust the messenger
That I should be attached in Ephesus.
I tell you, ’twill sound harshly in her ears.

Enter DROMIO OF EPHESUS with a rope’s end

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
sentences.text:
Here comes my man. I think he brings the money.
How now, sir? Have you that I sent you for?

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
sentences.text:
Here comes my man. I think he brings the money.
How now, sir? Have you that I sent you for?

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
sentences.text:
Here comes my man. I think he brings the money.
How now, sir? Have you that I sent you for?

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
sentences.text:
Here comes my man. I think he brings the money.
How now, sir? Have you that I sent you for?

sentences.speaker: DROMIO OF EPHESUS
sentences.text: Here’s that, I warrant you, will pay them all.

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
sentences.text: But where’s the money?

sentences.speaker: DROMIO OF EPHESUS
sentences.text: Why, sir, I gave the money for the rope.

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
sentences.text: Five hundred ducats, villain, for a rope?

sentences.speaker: DROMIO OF EPHESUS
sentences.text: I’ll serve you, sir, five hundred at the rate.

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
sentences.text: To what end did I bid thee hie thee home?

sentences.speaker: DROMIO OF EPHESUS
sentences.text: To a rope’s end, sir, and to that end am I returned.

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
sentences.text:
And to that end, sir, I will welcome you. [beats DROMIO
OF EPHESUS]

sentences.speaker: OFFICER
sentences.text: Good sir, be patient.

sentences.speaker: DROMIO OF EPHESUS
sentences.text: Nay, ’tis for me to be patient. I am in adversity.

sentences.speaker: DROMIO OF EPHESUS
sentences.text: Nay, ’tis for me to be patient. I am in adversity.

sentences.speaker: OFFICER
sentences.text: Good now, hold thy tongue.

sentences.speaker: DROMIO OF EPHESUS
sentences.text: Nay, rather persuade him to hold his hands.

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
sentences.text: Thou whoreson, senseless villain.

sentences.speaker: DROMIO OF EPHESUS
sentences.text:
I would I were senseless, sir, that I might not feel
your blows.

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
sentences.text:
Thou art sensible in nothing but blows, and so is an
ass.

sentences.speaker: DROMIO OF EPHESUS
sentences.text:
I am an ass, indeed; you may prove it by my long
ears.—I have served him from the hour of my nativity to
this instant, and have nothing at his hands for my
service but blows. When I am cold, he heats me with
beating; when I am warm, he cools me with beating. I am
waked with it when I sleep, raised with it when I sit,
driven out of doors with it when I go from home,
welcomed home with it when I return. Nay, I bear it on
my shoulders as a beggar wont her brat, and I think when
he hath lamed me, I shall beg with it from door to
door.

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
sentences.text: Come, go along. My wife is coming yonder.

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
sentences.text: Come, go along. My wife is coming yonder.
Enter ADRIANA, LUCIANA, the COURTESAN and a schoolmaster called PINCH

sentences.speaker: DROMIO OF EPHESUS
sentences.text:
Mistress, respice finem, respect your end, or rather,
the prophecy like the parrot, “Beware the rope’s end.”

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
sentences.text: Wilt thou still talk? [beats DROMIO OF EPHESUS]

sentences.speaker: COURTESAN
sentences.text: How say you now? Is not your husband mad?

sentences.speaker: COURTESAN
sentences.text: How say you now? Is not your husband mad?

sentences.speaker: ADRIANA
sentences.text:
His incivility confirms no less.—
Good Doctor Pinch, you are a conjurer;
Establish him in his true sense again,
And I will please you what you will demand.

sentences.speaker: ADRIANA
sentences.text:
His incivility confirms no less.—
Good Doctor Pinch, you are a conjurer;
Establish him in his true sense again,
And I will please you what you will demand.

sentences.speaker: ADRIANA
sentences.text:
His incivility confirms no less.—
Good Doctor Pinch, you are a conjurer;
Establish him in his true sense again,
And I will please you what you will demand.

sentences.speaker: LUCIANA
sentences.text: Alas, how fiery and how sharp he looks!

sentences.speaker: COURTESAN
sentences.text: Mark how he trembles in his ecstasy.

sentences.speaker: PINCH
sentences.text: Give me your hand, and let me feel your pulse.

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
sentences.text:
There is my hand, and let it feel your ear. [strikes
PINCH]

sentences.speaker: PINCH
sentences.text:
I charge thee, Satan, housed within this man,
To yield possession to my holy prayers
And to thy state of darkness hie thee straight.
I conjure thee by all the saints in heaven.

sentences.speaker: PINCH
sentences.text:
I charge thee, Satan, housed within this man,
To yield possession to my holy prayers
And to thy state of darkness hie thee straight.
I conjure thee by all the saints in heaven.

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
sentences.text: Peace, doting wizard, peace. I am not mad.

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
sentences.text: Peace, doting wizard, peace. I am not mad.

sentences.speaker: ADRIANA
sentences.text: O, that thou wert not, poor distressèd soul!

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
sentences.text:
You minion, you, are these your customers?
Did this companion with the saffron face
Revel and feast it at my house today
Whilst upon me the guilty doors were shut
And I denied to enter in my house?

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
sentences.text:
You minion, you, are these your customers?
Did this companion with the saffron face
Revel and feast it at my house today
Whilst upon me the guilty doors were shut
And I denied to enter in my house?

sentences.speaker: ADRIANA
sentences.text:
O husband, God doth know you dined at home,
Where would you had remained until this time,
Free from these slanders and this open shame.

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
sentences.text: “Dined at home”? Thou villain, what sayest thou?

sentences.speaker: DROMIO OF EPHESUS
sentences.text: Sir, sooth to say, you did not dine at home.

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
sentences.text: Were not my doors locked up and I shut out?

sentences.speaker: DROMIO OF EPHESUS
sentences.text: Perdie, your doors were locked, and you shut out.

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
sentences.text: And did not she herself revile me there?

sentences.speaker: DROMIO OF EPHESUS
sentences.text: Sans fable, she herself reviled you there.

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
sentences.text: Did not her kitchen maid rail, taunt, and scorn me?

sentences.speaker: DROMIO OF EPHESUS
sentences.text: Certes, she did; the kitchen vestal scorned you.

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
sentences.text: And did not I in rage depart from thence?

sentences.speaker: DROMIO OF EPHESUS
sentences.text:
In verity you did.—My bones bear witness,
That since have felt the vigour of his rage.

sentences.speaker: ADRIANA
sentences.text: [To PINCH] Is’t good to soothe him in these contraries?

sentences.speaker: PINCH
sentences.text:
It is no shame. The fellow finds his vein
And, yielding to him, humors well his frenzy.

sentences.speaker: PINCH
sentences.text:
It is no shame. The fellow finds his vein
And, yielding to him, humors well his frenzy.

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
sentences.text:
[to ADRIANA ] Thou hast suborned the goldsmith to arrest
me.

sentences.speaker: ADRIANA
sentences.text:
Alas, I sent you money to redeem you
By Dromio here, who came in haste for it.

sentences.speaker: DROMIO OF EPHESUS
sentences.text:
Money by me! heart and goodwill you might,
But surely, master, not a rag of money.

sentences.speaker: DROMIO OF EPHESUS
sentences.text:
Money by me! heart and goodwill you might,
But surely, master, not a rag of money.

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
sentences.text: Went’st not thou to her for a purse of ducats?

sentences.speaker: ADRIANA
sentences.text: He came to me, and I delivered it.

sentences.speaker: LUCIANA
sentences.text: And I am witness with her that she did.

sentences.speaker: DROMIO OF EPHESUS
sentences.text:
God and the rope-maker bear me witness
That I was sent for nothing but a rope.

sentences.speaker: PINCH
sentences.text:
Mistress, both man and master is possessed.
I know it by their pale and deadly looks.
They must be bound and laid in some dark room.

sentences.speaker: PINCH
sentences.text:
Mistress, both man and master is possessed.
I know it by their pale and deadly looks.
They must be bound and laid in some dark room.

sentences.speaker: PINCH
sentences.text:
Mistress, both man and master is possessed.
I know it by their pale and deadly looks.
They must be bound and laid in some dark room.

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
sentences.text:
[to ADRIANA ] Say wherefore didst thou lock me forth
today.
[to DROMIO OF EPHESUS ] And why dost thou deny the bag
of gold?

sentences.speaker: ADRIANA
sentences.text: I did not, gentle husband, lock thee forth.

sentences.speaker: DROMIO OF EPHESUS
sentences.text:
And, gentle master, I received no gold.
But I confess, sir, that we were locked out.

sentences.speaker: DROMIO OF EPHESUS
sentences.text:
And, gentle master, I received no gold.
But I confess, sir, that we were locked out.

sentences.speaker: ADRIANA
sentences.text: Dissembling villain, thou speak’st false in both.

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
sentences.text:
Dissembling harlot, thou art false in all,
And art confederate with a damnèd pack
To make a loathsome abject scorn of me.
But with these nails I’ll pluck out these false eyes
That would behold in me this shameful sport.

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS

Enter three or four, and offer to bind him. He strives.
Enter three or four, and offer to bind him. He strives.

sentences.speaker: ADRIANA
sentences.text: O bind him, bind him! Let him not come near me.

sentences.speaker: ADRIANA
sentences.text: O bind him, bind him! Let him not come near me.

sentences.speaker: PINCH
sentences.text: More company! The fiend is strong within him.

sentences.speaker: PINCH
sentences.text: More company! The fiend is strong within him.

sentences.speaker: LUCIANA
sentences.text: Ay me, poor man, how pale and wan he looks!

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
sentences.text:
What, will you murder me?—Thou jailer, thou,
I am thy prisoner. Wilt thou suffer them
To make a rescue?

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
sentences.text:
What, will you murder me?—Thou jailer, thou,
I am thy prisoner. Wilt thou suffer them
To make a rescue?

sentences.speaker: OFFICER
sentences.text:
Masters, let him go.
He is my prisoner, and you shall not have him.

sentences.speaker: OFFICER
sentences.text:
Masters, let him go.
He is my prisoner, and you shall not have him.

sentences.speaker: PINCH
sentences.text: Go, bind this man, for he is frantic too.

sentences.speaker: PINCH
sentences.text: Go, bind this man, for he is frantic too.

sentences.speaker: ADRIANA
sentences.text:
What wilt thou do, thou peevish officer?
Hast thou delight to see a wretched man
Do outrage and displeasure to himself?

sentences.speaker: ADRIANA
sentences.text:
What wilt thou do, thou peevish officer?
Hast thou delight to see a wretched man
Do outrage and displeasure to himself?

sentences.speaker: OFFICER
sentences.text:
He is my prisoner. If I let him go,
The debt he owes will be required of me.

sentences.speaker: OFFICER
sentences.text:
He is my prisoner. If I let him go,
The debt he owes will be required of me.

sentences.speaker: ADRIANA
sentences.text:
I will discharge thee ere I go from thee.
Bear me forthwith unto his creditor,
And, knowing how the debt grows, I will pay it.—
Good Master Doctor, see him safe conveyed
Home to my house. O most unhappy day!

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
sentences.text: O most unhappy strumpet!

sentences.speaker: DROMIO OF EPHESUS
sentences.text: Master, I am here entered in bond for you.

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
sentences.text: Out on thee, villain! Wherefore dost thou mad me?

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
sentences.text: Out on thee, villain! Wherefore dost thou mad me?

sentences.speaker: DROMIO OF EPHESUS
sentences.text:
Will you be bound for nothing? Be mad, good master.
Cry “The devil!”

sentences.speaker: DROMIO OF EPHESUS
sentences.text:

Cry “The devil!”

sentences.speaker: DROMIO OF EPHESUS
sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: LUCIANA
sentences.text: God help poor souls! How idly do they talk!

sentences.speaker: LUCIANA
sentences.text: God help poor souls! How idly do they talk!

sentences.speaker: ADRIANA
sentences.text: Go bear him hence. Sister, go you with me.

sentences.speaker: ADRIANA
sentences.text: Go bear him hence. Sister, go you with me.

sentences.speaker: OFFICER
sentences.text: One Angelo, a goldsmith. Do you know him?

sentences.speaker: OFFICER
sentences.text: One Angelo, a goldsmith. Do you know him?

sentences.speaker: ADRIANA
sentences.text: I know the man. What is the sum he owes?

sentences.speaker: ADRIANA
sentences.text: I know the man. What is the sum he owes?

sentences.speaker: OFFICER
sentences.text: Two hundred ducats.

sentences.speaker: ADRIANA
sentences.text: Say, how grows it due?

sentences.speaker: OFFICER
sentences.text: Due for a chain your husband had of him.

sentences.speaker: ADRIANA
sentences.text: He did bespeak a chain for me but had it not.

sentences.speaker: COURTESAN
sentences.text:
Whenas your husband all in rage today
Came to my house and took away my ring,
The ring I saw upon his finger now,
Straight after did I meet him with a chain.

sentences.speaker: ADRIANA
sentences.text:
It may be so, but I did never see it.—
Come, jailer, bring me where the goldsmith is.
I long to know the truth hereof at large.

sentences.speaker: ADRIANA

sentences.speaker: LUCIANA
sentences.text: God for Thy mercy, they are loose again!

sentences.speaker: ADRIANA
sentences.text:
And come with naked swords. Let’s call more help
To have them bound again.

sentences.speaker: ADRIANA
sentences.text:
And come with naked swords. Let’s call more help

sentences.speaker: OFFICER
sentences.text: Away! They’ll kill us.

sentences.speaker: OFFICER
sentences.text: Away! They’ll kill us.

sentences.text: Away! They’ll kill us.

sentences.speaker: OFFICER

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text: I see these witches are afraid of swords.

sentences.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text: She that would be your wife now ran from you.

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text:
Come to the Centaur. Fetch our stuff from thence.
I long that we were safe and sound aboard.

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text:
Come to the Centaur. Fetch our stuff from thence.
I long that we were safe and sound aboard.

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text:
Come to the Centaur. Fetch our stuff from thence.
I long that we were safe and sound aboard.

sentences.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text:
Faith, stay here this night. They will surely do us no
harm. You saw they speak us fair, give us gold. Methinks
they are such a gentle nation that, but for the
mountain of mad flesh that claims marriage of me, i
could find in my heart to stay here still, and turn
witch.

sentences.text:
I will not stay tonight for all the town.
Therefore away, to get our stuff aboard.

Enter SECOND MERCHANT and ANGELO the goldsmith

sentences.speaker: ANGELO
sentences.text:
I am sorry, sir, that I have hindered you,
But I protest he had the chain of me,
Though most dishonestly he doth deny it.

sentences.speaker: SECOND MERCHANT
sentences.text: How is the man esteemed here in the city?

sentences.speaker: ANGELO
sentences.text:
Of very reverend reputation, sir,
Of credit infinite, highly beloved,
Second to none that lives here in the city.
His word might bear my wealth at any time.

sentences.speaker: ANGELO
sentences.text:
Of very reverend reputation, sir,

Second to none that lives here in the city.
His word might bear my wealth at any time.

sentences.speaker: SECOND MERCHANT
sentences.text: Speak softly. Yonder, as I think, he walks.

sentences.speaker: ANGELO
sentences.text:
'Tis so; and that self chain about his neck
Which he forswore most monstrously to have.
Good sir, draw near to me. I’ll speak to him.—
Signior Antipholus, I wonder much
That you would put me to this shame and trouble,
And not without some scandal to yourself,
With circumstance and oaths so to deny
This chain, which now you wear so openly.
Beside the charge, the shame, imprisonment,
You have done wrong to this my honest friend,
Who, but for staying on our controversy,
Had hoisted sail and put to sea today.
This chain you had of me. Can you deny it?

sentences.text: I think I had. I never did deny it.

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text: I think I had. I never did deny it.

sentences.speaker: SECOND MERCHANT
sentences.text: Yes, that you did, sir, and forswore it too.

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text: Who heard me to deny it or forswear it?

sentences.speaker: SECOND MERCHANT
sentences.text:
These ears of mine, thou know’st did hear thee.
Fie on thee, wretch. 'Tis pity that thou liv’st
To walk where any honest men resort.

sentences.speaker: SECOND MERCHANT
sentences.text:

To walk where any honest men resort.

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text:
Thou art a villain to impeach me thus.
I’ll prove mine honor and mine honesty
Against thee presently if thou dar’st stand.

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text:
Thou art a villain to impeach me thus.
I’ll prove mine honor and mine honesty
Against thee presently if thou dar’st stand.

sentences.speaker: SECOND MERCHANT
sentences.text: I dare, and do defy thee for a villain.

sentences.text:
Hold, hurt him not, for God’s sake. He is mad.—
Some get within him; take his sword away.
Bind dromio too, and bear them to my house!

Run, master, run. For God’s sake, take a house.
This is some priory. In, or we are spoiled.

Enter the Lady ABBESS

sentences.speaker: ABBESS
sentences.text: Be quiet, people. Wherefore throng you hither?

sentences.speaker: ABBESS
sentences.text: Be quiet, people. Wherefore throng you hither?

sentences.speaker: ADRIANA
sentences.text:
To fetch my poor distracted husband hence.
Let us come in, that we may bind him fast
And bear him home for his recovery.

sentences.speaker: ADRIANA
sentences.text:
To fetch my poor distracted husband hence.
Let us come in, that we may bind him fast
And bear him home for his recovery.

sentences.speaker: ANGELO
sentences.text: I knew he was not in his perfect wits.

sentences.speaker: SECOND MERCHANT
sentences.text: I am sorry now that I did draw on him.

sentences.speaker: ABBESS
sentences.text: How long hath this possession held the man?

sentences.speaker: ADRIANA
sentences.text:
This week he hath been heavy, sour, sad,
And much different from the man he was.
But till this afternoon his passion
Ne'er brake into extremity of rage.

sentences.speaker: ADRIANA
sentences.text:
This week he hath been heavy, sour, sad,
And much different from the man he was.
But till this afternoon his passion
Ne'er brake into extremity of rage.

sentences.speaker: ABBESS
sentences.text:
Hath he not lost much wealth by wrack of sea?
Buried some dear friend? Hath not else his eye
Stray’d his affection in unlawful love,
A sin prevailing much in youthful men
Who give their eyes the liberty of gazing?
Which of these sorrows is he subject to?

Namely, some love that drew him oft from home.

sentences.speaker: ADRIANA
sentences.text: Why, so I did.

sentences.speaker: ABBESS
sentences.text: Ay, but not rough enough.

sentences.speaker: ADRIANA

sentences.speaker: ABBESS

ABBESS
sentences.text:
And thereof came it that the man was mad.
The venom clamors of a jealous woman
Poisons more deadly than a mad dog’s tooth.
It seems his sleeps were hinder’d by thy railing,
And therefore comes it that his head is light.
Thou sayst his meat was sauced with thy upbraidings.
Unquiet meals make ill digestions.
Thereof the raging fire of fever bred,
And what’s a fever but a fit of madness?
Thou sayest his sports were hinderd by thy brawls.
Sweet recreation barred, what doth ensue
But moody and dull melancholy,
Kinsman to grim and comfortless despair,
And at her heels a huge infectious troop
Of pale distemperatures and foes to life?
In food, in sport, and life-preserving rest
To be disturbed, would mad or man or beast.
The consequence is, then, thy jealous fits
Have scared thy husband from the use of wits.

The consequence is, then, thy jealous fits
Have scared thy husband from the use of wits.

sentences.speaker: ABBESS
sentences.text:
And thereof came it that the man was mad.
The venom clamors of a jealous woman
Poisons more deadly than a mad dog’s tooth.
It seems his sleeps were hinder’d by thy railing,
And therefore comes it that his head is light.
Thou sayst his meat was sauced with thy upbraidings.
Unquiet meals make ill digestions.
Thereof the raging fire of fever bred,
And what’s a fever but a fit of madness?
Thou sayest his sports were hinderd by thy brawls.
Sweet recreation barred, what doth ensue
But moody and dull melancholy,
Kinsman to grim and comfortless despair,
And at her heels a huge infectious troop
Of pale distemperatures and foes to life?
In food, in sport, and life-preserving rest
To be disturbed, would mad or man or beast.
The consequence is, then, thy jealous fits
Have scared thy husband from the use of wits.

sentences.speaker: LUCIANA
sentences.text:
She never reprehended him but mildly
When he demeaned himself rough, rude, and wildly.—
[to ADRIANA] Why bear you these rebukes and answer not?

sentences.speaker: ADRIANA
sentences.text:
She did betray me to my own reproof.
Good people, enter and lay hold on him.

sentences.speaker: ADRIANA
sentences.text:
She did betray me to my own reproof.
Good people, enter and lay hold on him.

sentences.speaker: ABBESS
sentences.text: No, not a creature enters in my house.

sentences.speaker: ADRIANA
sentences.text: Then let your servants bring my husband forth.

sentences.speaker: ABBESS
sentences.text:
Neither: he took this place for sanctuary,
And it shall privilege him from your hands
Till I have brought him to his wits again
Or lose my labor in assaying it.

sentences.speaker: ABBESS
sentences.text:
Neither: he took this place for sanctuary,
And it shall privilege him from your hands
Till I have brought him to his wits again
Or lose my labor in assaying it.

sentences.speaker: ADRIANA
sentences.text:
I will attend my husband, be his nurse,
Diet his sickness, for it is my office
And will have no attorney but myself;
And therefore let me have him home with me.

sentences.speaker: ADRIANA
sentences.text:

And therefore let me have him home with me.

sentences.speaker: ABBESS
sentences.text:
Be patient, for I will not let him stir
Till I have used the approvd means I have,
With wholesome syrups, drugs, and holy prayers,
To make of him a formal man again.
It is a branch and parcel of mine oath,
A charitable duty of my order.
Therefore depart and leave him here with me.

And never rise until my tears and prayers
Have won his grace to come in person hither
And take perforce my husband from the Abbess.

sentences.speaker: SECOND MERCHANT
sentences.text:
By this, I think, the dial points at five.
Anon, I’m sure, the Duke himself in person
Comes this way to the melancholy vale,
The place of death and sorry execution
Behind the ditches of the abbey here.

sentences.speaker: ANGELO
sentences.text: Upon what cause?

sentences.speaker: SECOND MERCHANT
sentences.text:
To see a reverend Syracusian merchant,
Who put unluckily into this bay
Against the laws and statutes of this town,
Beheaded publicly for his offense.

sentences.speaker: ANGELO
sentences.text: See where they come. We will behold his death.

sentences.speaker: ANGELO
sentences.text: See where they come. We will behold his death.

sentences.speaker: LUCIANA
sentences.text: Kneel to the duke before he pass the abbey.
Enter the DUKE OF EPHESUS and EGEON the merchant of Syracuse, bare head, with the headsman and other officers

sentences.speaker: DUKE
sentences.text:
Yet once again proclaim it publicly,
If any friend will pay the sum for him,
He shall not die; so much we tender him.

sentences.speaker: DUKE
sentences.text:
Yet once again proclaim it publicly,
If any friend will pay the sum for him,
He shall not die; so much we tender him.

sentences.speaker: ADRIANA
sentences.text: Justice, most sacred duke, against the Abbess.

sentences.speaker: DUKE
sentences.text:
She is a virtuous and a reverend lady.
It cannot be that she hath done thee wrong.

sentences.speaker: DUKE
sentences.text:
She is a virtuous and a reverend lady.
It cannot be that she hath done thee wrong.

sentences.speaker: ADRIANA
sentences.text:
May it please your Grace, Antipholus my husband,
Whom I made lord of me and all I had
At your important letters, this ill day
A most outrageous fit of madness took him,
That desp'rately he hurried through the street,
With him his bondman, all as mad as he,
Doing displeasure to the citizens
By rushing in their houses, bearing thence
Rings, jewels, any thing his rage did like.
Once did I get him bound and sent him home
Whilst to take order for the wrongs I went
That here and there his fury had committed.
Anon, I wot not by what strong escape,
He broke from those that had the guard of him,
And with his mad attendant and himself,
Each one with ireful passion, with drawn swords,
Met us again and, madly bent on us,
Chased us away, till, raising of more aid,
We came again to bind them. Then they fled
Into this abbey, whither we pursued them,
And here the Abbess shuts the gates on us
And will not suffer us to fetch him out,
Nor send him forth that we may bear him hence.
Therefore, most gracious duke, with thy command
Let him be brought forth and borne hence for help.

sentences.speaker: DUKE
sentences.text:
Long since thy husband served me in my wars,
And I to thee engaged a prince’s word,
When thou didst make him master of thy bed,
To do him all the grace and good I could.
Go, some of you, knock at the abbey gate,
And bid the Lady Abbess come to me.
I will determine this before I stir.

Enter a MESSENGER

sentences.speaker: MESSENGER
sentences.text:
O mistress, mistress, shift and save yourself.
My master and his man are both broke loose,
Beaten the maids a-row, and bound the doctor,
Whose beard they have singed off with brands of fire,
And ever as it blazed, they threw on him
Great pails of puddled mire to quench the hair.
My master preaches patience to him, and the while
His man with scissors nicks him like a fool;
And sure, unless you send some present help,
Between them they will kill the conjurer.

sentences.speaker: ADRIANA

Peace, fool. Thy master and his man are here,
And that is false thou dost report to us.

sentences.speaker: ADRIANA

sentences.speaker: MESSENGER
sentences.text:
Mistress, upon my life I tell you true.
I have not breathed almost since I did see it.
He cries for you, and vows, if he can take you,
To scorch your face and to disfigure you.

sentences.speaker: ADRIANA

Ay me, it is my husband. Witness you
That he is borne about invisible.
Even now we housed him in the abbey here,
And now he’s there, past thought of human reason.

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS

Justice, most gracious duke, O, grant me justice,
Even for the service that long since I did thee
When I bestrid thee in the wars and took
Deep scars to save thy life. Even for the blood
That then I lost for thee, now grant me justice.

sentences.speaker: EGEON
sentences.text:
[aside] Unless the fear of death doth make me dote,
I see my son Antipholus and Dromio.

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
sentences.text:
Justice, sweet prince, against that woman there,
She whom thou gav’st to me to be my wife,
That hath abusèd and dishonored me
Even in the strength and height of injury.
Beyond imagination is the wrong
That she this day hath shameless thrown on me.

sentences.speaker: DUKE
sentences.text: Discover how, and thou shalt find me just.

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
sentences.text:
This day, great duke, she shut the doors upon me
While she with harlots feasted in my house.

sentences.speaker: DUKE
sentences.text: A grievous fault.—Say, woman, didst thou so?

sentences.speaker: ADRIANA
sentences.text:
No, my good lord. Myself, he, and my sister
Today did dine together. So befall my soul
As this is false he burdens me withal.

sentences.speaker: ADRIANA

sentences.speaker: LUCIANA
sentences.text:
Ne'er may I look on day, nor sleep on night
But she tells to your Highness simple truth.

sentences.speaker: ANGELO
sentences.text:
O perjured woman! —They are both forsworn.
In this the madman justly chargeth them.

sentences.speaker: ANGELO
sentences.text:

In this the madman justly chargeth them.

sentences.speaker: ANGELO

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
sentences.text:
My liege, I am advisèd what I say,
Neither disturbed with the effect of wine,
Nor heady-rash, provoked with raging ire,
Albeit my wrongs might make one wiser mad.
This woman locked me out this day from dinner.
That goldsmith there, were he not packed with her,
Could witness it, for he was with me then,
Who parted with me to go fetch a chain,
Promising to bring it to the Porpentine,
Where Balthasar and I did dine together.

I went to seek him. In the street I met him,
And in his company that gentleman. [points to the
SECOND MERCHANT ]

Then fairly I bespoke the officer
To go in person with me to my house.

Of vile confederates. Along with them
They brought one Pinch, a hungry, lean-faced villain,
A mere anatomy, a mountebank,
A threadbare juggler, and a fortune-teller,
A needy, hollow-eyed, sharp-looking wretch,
A living dead man. This pernicious slave,
Forsooth, took on him as a conjurer,
And, gazing in mine eyes, feeling my pulse,
And with no face (as ’twere) outfacing me,
Cries out I was possessed. Then all together
They fell upon me, bound me, bore me thence,
And in a dark and dankish vault at home
There left me and my man, both bound together,
Till gnawing with my teeth my bonds in sunder,
I gained my freedom and immediately
Ran hither to your Grace, whom I beseech
To give me ample satisfaction
For these deep shames and great indignities.

And with no face (as ’twere) outfacing me,
Cries out I was possessed. Then all together
They fell upon me, bound me, bore me thence,
And in a dark and dankish vault at home
There left me and my man, both bound together,
Till gnawing with my teeth my bonds in sunder,
I gained my freedom and immediately
Ran hither to your Grace, whom I beseech
To give me ample satisfaction
For these deep shames and great indignities.

sentences.speaker: ANGELO
sentences.text:
My lord, in truth, thus far I witness with him:
That he dined not at home, but was locked out.

sentences.speaker: DUKE
sentences.text: But had he such a chain of thee or no?

sentences.speaker: ANGELO
sentences.text:
He had, my lord, and when he ran in here,
These people saw the chain about his neck.

sentences.speaker: SECOND MERCHANT
sentences.text:
Besides, I will be sworn these ears of mine
Heard you confess you had the chain of him
After you first forswore it on the mart,
And thereupon I drew my sword on you,
And then you fled into this abbey here,
From whence I think you are come by miracle.

sentences.speaker: SECOND MERCHANT
sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
sentences.text:
I never came within these abbey walls,
Nor ever didst thou draw thy sword on me.
I never saw the chain, so help me heaven,
And this is false you burden me withal.

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: DUKE
sentences.text:
Why, what an intricate impeach is this!
I think you all have drunk of Circe’s cup.
If here you housed him, here he would have been.
If he were mad, he would not plead so coldly.
[to ADRIANA ] You say he dined at home; the goldsmith
here
Denies that saying. Sirrah, what say you?

sentences.speaker: COURTESAN
sentences.text: He did, and from my finger snatched that ring.

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
sentences.text: 'Tis true, my liege; this ring I had of her.

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
sentences.text: 'Tis true, my liege; this ring I had of her.

sentences.speaker: DUKE
sentences.text: Saw’st thou him enter at the abbey here?

sentences.speaker: COURTESAN
sentences.text: As sure, my liege, as I do see your Grace.

sentences.speaker: DUKE
sentences.text:
Why, this is strange.—Go call the Abbess hither.
I think you are all mated or stark mad.

sentences.speaker: DUKE

sentences.speaker: EGEON
sentences.text:
Most mighty duke, vouchsafe me speak a word.
Haply I see a friend will save my life
And pay the sum that may deliver me.

sentences.speaker: EGEON
sentences.text:
Most mighty duke, vouchsafe me speak a word.

And pay the sum that may deliver me.

sentences.speaker: DUKE
sentences.text: Speak freely, Syracusian, what thou wilt.

sentences.speaker: EGEON
sentences.text:
[to ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS ] Is not your name, sir,
called
Antipholus?
And is not that your bondman Dromio?

sentences.speaker: EGEON
sentences.text:
[to ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS ] Is not your name, sir,
called
Antipholus?
And is not that your bondman Dromio?

Within this hour I was his bondman sir,
But he, I thank him, gnawed in two my cords.
Now am I Dromio, and his man, unbound.

sentences.speaker: EGEON
sentences.text: I am sure you both of you remember me.

sentences.speaker: DROMIO OF EPHESUS
sentences.text:
Ourselves we do remember, sir, by you.
For lately we were bound as you are now.
You are not Pinch’s patient, are you, sir?

sentences.speaker: DROMIO OF EPHESUS
sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: EGEON
sentences.text: Why look you strange on me? you know me well.

sentences.speaker: EGEON
sentences.text: Why look you strange on me? you know me well.

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
sentences.text: I never saw you in my life till now.

sentences.speaker: EGEON
sentences.text:
O, grief hath changed me since you saw me last,
And careful hours with time’s deformèd hand
Have written strange defeatures in my face.
But tell me yet, dost thou not know my voice?

sentences.speaker: EGEON
sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: EGEON

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
sentences.text: Neither.

sentences.speaker: EGEON
sentences.text: Dromio, nor thou?

sentences.speaker: DROMIO OF EPHESUS
sentences.text: No, trust me, sir, nor I.

sentences.speaker: EGEON
sentences.text: I am sure thou dost.

sentences.speaker: DROMIO OF EPHESUS
sentences.text:
Ay, sir, but I am sure I do not, and whatsoever a man
denies, you are now bound to believe him.

sentences.speaker: EGEON
sentences.text:
Not know my voice! O time’s extremity,
Hast thou so crack’d and splitted my poor tongue
In seven short years that here my only son
Knows not my feeble key of untuned cares?
Though now this grainèd face of mine be hid
In sap-consuming winter’s drizzled snow,
And all the conduits of my blood froze up,
Yet hath my night of life some memory,
My wasting lamps some fading glimmer left,
My dull deaf ears a little use to hear.
All these old witnesses—I cannot err—
Tell me thou art my son Antipholus.

sentences.text: I never saw my father in my life.

sentences.speaker: EGEON
sentences.text:
But seven years since, in Syracusa, boy,
Thou know’st we parted. But perhaps, my son,
Thou sham’st to acknowledge me in misery.

sentences.speaker: EGEON

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
sentences.text:
The Duke and all that know me in the city
Can witness with me that it is not so
I ne'er saw Syracusa in my life.

sentences.speaker: DUKE
sentences.text:
I tell thee, Syracusian, twenty years
Have I been patron to Antipholus,
During which time he ne'er saw Syracusa.
I see thy age and dangers make thee dote.

sentences.speaker: ABBESS

sentences.speaker: ADRIANA

sentences.speaker: DUKE
sentences.text:
[looks at the ANTIPHOLUS tw ins] One of these men is
genius to the other.
[looks at the DROMIO tw ins] And so, of these, which is
the natural man
And which the spirit? Who deciphers them?

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text: Egeon art thou not, or else his ghost?

sentences.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text: O, my old master.—Who hath bound him here?

sentences.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text: O, my old master.—Who hath bound him here?

sentences.speaker: ABBESS
sentences.text:
Whoever bound him, I will loose his bonds
And gain a husband by his liberty.—
Speak, old Egeon, if thou be’st the man
That hadst a wife once called Emilia,
That bore thee at a burden two fair sons.
O, if thou be’st the same Egeon, speak,
And speak unto the same Emilia.

sentences.speaker: DUKE
sentences.text:
Why, here begins his morning story right;
These two Antipholuses, these two so like,
And these two Dromios, one in semblance—
Besides his urging of her wreck at sea—
These are the parents to these children,
Which accidentally are met together.

sentences.speaker: EGEON
sentences.text:
If I dream not, thou art Emilia.
If thou art she, tell me where is that son
That floated with thee on the fatal raft?

sentences.speaker: EGEON
sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: ABBESS
sentences.text:
By men of Epidamnum he and I
And the twin Dromio all were taken up;
But by and by rude fishermen of Corinth
By force took Dromio and my son from them
And me they left with those of Epidamnum.
What then became of them I cannot tell;
I to this fortune that you see me in.

from
Corinth first.

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text: No, sir, not I. I came from Syracuse.

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE

sentences.speaker: DUKE
sentences.text: Stay, stand apart. I know not which is which.

sentences.speaker: DUKE
sentences.text: Stay, stand apart. I know not which is which.

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
sentences.text: I came from Corinth, my most gracious lord.

sentences.speaker: DROMIO OF EPHESUS
sentences.text: And I with him.

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
sentences.text:
Brought to this town by that most famous warrior
Duke Menaphon, your most renownèd uncle.

sentences.speaker: ADRIANA
sentences.text: Which of you two did dine with me today?

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text: I, gentle mistress.

sentences.speaker: ADRIANA
sentences.text: And are not you my husband?

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
sentences.text: No, I say nay to that.

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text:
And so do I, yet did she call me so,
And this fair gentlewoman, her sister here,
Did call me brother. [t o LUCIANA ] What I told you then
I hope I shall have leisure to make good,
If this be not a dream I see and hear.

sentences.speaker: ANGELO
sentences.text: That is the chain, sir, which you had of me.

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text: I think it be, sir. I deny it not.

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text: I think it be, sir. I deny it not.

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
sentences.text: And you, sir, for this chain arrested me.

sentences.speaker: ANGELO
sentences.text: I think I did, sir. I deny it not.

sentences.speaker: ANGELO
sentences.text: I think I did, sir. I deny it not.

sentences.speaker: ADRIANA
sentences.text:
I sent you money, sir, to be your bail
By Dromio, but I think he brought it not.

sentences.speaker: DROMIO OF EPHESUS
sentences.text: No, none by me.

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text:
This purse of ducats I received from you,
And Dromio my man did bring them me.
I see we still did meet each other’s man,
And I was ta'en for him, and he for me,
And thereupon these errors are arose.

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
sentences.text: These ducats pawn I for my father here.

sentences.speaker: DUKE
sentences.text: It shall not need. Thy father hath his life.

sentences.speaker: DUKE
sentences.text: It shall not need. Thy father hath his life.

sentences.speaker: COURTESAN
sentences.text: Sir, I must have that diamond from you.

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
sentences.text: There, take it; and much thanks for my good cheer.

sentences.speaker: ABBESS
sentences.text:
Renownèd duke, vouchsafe to take the pains
To go with us into the abbey here
And hear at large discoursed all our fortunes,
And all that are assembled in this place
That by this sympathizèd one day’s error
Have suffered wrong. Go, keep us company,
And we shall make full satisfaction.—
Thirty-three years have I but gone in travail
Of you, my sons, and till this present hour
My heavy burden ne'er deliverèd.—
The Duke, my husband, and my children both,
And you, the calendars of their nativity,
Go to a gossips' feast, and go with me.
After so long grief, such nativity!

sentences.speaker: DUKE
sentences.text: With all my heart I’ll gossip at this feast.
Exeunt; the two DROMIOS and the two ANTIPHOLUS brothers remain behind.

sentences.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE

[to ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS ] Master, shall I fetch your
stuff from shipboard?

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF EPHESUS
sentences.text: Dromio, what stuff of mine hast thou embarked?

sentences.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text: Your goods that lay at host, sir, in the Centaur.

sentences.speaker: ANTIPHOLUS OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text:
He speaks to me.—I am your master, Dromio.
Come, go with us. We’ll look to that anon.
Embrace thy brother there. Rejoice with him.

sentences.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text:
There is a fat friend at your master’s house
That kitchened me for you today at dinner.
She now shall be my sister, not my wife.

There is a fat friend at your master’s house
That kitchened me for you today at dinner.
She now shall be my sister, not my wife.

sentences.speaker: DROMIO OF EPHESUS
sentences.text:
Methinks you are my glass, and not my brother:
I see by you I am a sweet-faced youth.
Will you walk in to see their gossiping?

sentences.speaker: DROMIO OF EPHESUS
sentences.text:
Methinks you are my glass, and not my brother:
I see by you I am a sweet-faced youth.
Will you walk in to see their gossiping?

sentences.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text: Not I, sir. You are my elder.

sentences.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text: Not I, sir. You are my elder.

sentences.speaker: DROMIO OF EPHESUS
sentences.text: That’s a question. How shall we try it?

sentences.speaker: DROMIO OF EPHESUS
sentences.text: That’s a question. How shall we try it?

sentences.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text:
We’ll draw cuts for the signior. Till then, lead thou
first.

sentences.speaker: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE
sentences.text:
We’ll draw cuts for the signior. Till then, lead thou
first.

sentences.speaker: DROMIO OF EPHESUS
sentences.text:
Nay, then, thus:
We came into the world like brother and brother,
And now let’s go hand in hand, not one before another.
Exeunt
